name: Indochine Premium Cruise
address: Ô 21 Bến Cảng Quốc Tế, Tuần Châu, Quảng Ninh 200000, Vietnam
text: The food was magnificent, the staff was very attentive, and the rooms were beautiful. The only downside, which I wish we had known beforehand, was having to leave the cruise for 7 hours during the 2-day tour. It makes more sense to book a single-day tour. While the 2-day option is enjoyable, you really need to check the itinerary details and ask questions; being transferred to a different boat for 7-8 hours is quite ridiculous because it ruins the comfort of the main cruise.

name: Burger Street Urla
text: The burger was pretty good. Not amazing enough to say "I'd eat here every week," but when you do eat it, it definitely won't disappoint. Having beer on the menu is also a nice plus. The fries could maybe use some improvement.

name: Dokuz Pub
text: The new place turned out really nice, close to the sea, and the atmosphere is quite suitable for daytime hangouts too.

name: Urla İskele Berlins
text: One of the best bars in Urla. We didn't try the food but it's a great spot for hanging out in the evening. Good music, nice atmosphere. A proper bar.

name: The HARP Irish Pub | Urla
text: Nice pool table setup, a fun pub.

name: Han Buldan - Wine House
text:
The wine menu is quite extensive, the food is tasty, and the ambiance is really enjoyable. It's worth making a reservation ahead of time since it gets crowded on weekends. They apparently prepare the pizza dough based on reservations, so it could be a problem if you show up without one.

The service is just a bit slow, but other than that everything is quite enjoyable.

name: Hayat Urla
text:
The atmosphere is quite nice, but the insulation isn't great for winter days; it was a bit cold inside despite the heater running.

For breakfast there's a single menu in a spread-style breakfast format, and you can also order eggs on the side. There's a separate menu for kids too.

Overall the product quality is nice, and the content wasn't bad. The breakfast is pretty classic though, nothing innovative. They've chosen to go with that concept. It would've been better if the menu were a bit richer with more alternatives.

name: Lima Peru Urla
address: Yalı Caddesi, İskele, 2126. Sk. No15, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text:
Overall the service and food quality are good. The only issue is that the place is a bit small, so there's a general ventilation problem, especially in winter, food smells really cling to you.

Choritos A la Chalaca was our favorite item, especially the sauce which was excellent. The seafood platter was a bit mixed and too oily. The noodles were too salty.

It was quite disappointing that the wine options were so limited. While the menu lists 4 different white wine options, in reality there were only 2.

Still, if you're into regional cuisine, it's definitely worth a try.

name: FooWo Cafe&Library
text: Good coffees, reasonable prices.

name: Ulus Cafe
address: Kuruçeşme Adnan Saygun Cad, Kuruçeşme, Ulus Parkı 71/B, 34345 Beşiktaş/İstanbul
text: The view and atmosphere are amazing. A really nice spot to sit and have tea or coffee.

name: Patisserie Nebiyye
text: Desserts are pretty good, comfortable atmosphere.

name: Miske Restaurant
address: Siteler, Bursa Cd. No:35/A, 20160 Denizli Merkezefendi/Denizli
text: Food quality is still good, but the service has slowed down a bit and the music has changed a lot. The music definitely doesn't fit the vibe; they were playing songs like it was a nightclub. Good that the food is still tasty, and I think the prices are reasonable, but they could use some organizational fine-tuning.

name: Lâdika
text: The staff are very friendly, the ambiance is nice, and we had dessert. It wasn't bad. Worth a try.

name: Claros Bağcılık ve Şarapçılık
text: A winery I'd recommend to anyone passing through the area. Especially when they do their taco events in summer, eating and drinking wine there is such a great time. The ambiance is wonderful.

name: Batis
text:
The place is really beautiful, especially the interior ambiance is impressive. The cocktails are a bit too strong; they definitely don't skimp on ingredients, but the alcohol taste overpowers. The food isn't bad but the prices are a notch high overall.

Edit from years later: They've really improved things for both breakfast and dinner. We definitely recommend it for clean, quality food. The club member thing also saves you good money.

name: Urladam
text:
The place is truly beautiful and what they're trying to do is really eye-catching, but unfortunately it's a venue with management issues. Problems keep coming up both with events and running the cafe itself. I hope they can sort it out.

In winter, despite no smoking being allowed inside the cafe area, so much cigarette smoke drifts in from the semi-enclosed section that it's absolute torture for non-smokers.

name: Özsüt Select
text: Desserts are quite good, prices are reasonable for food and desserts but ridiculous for alcohol. A kid-friendly place where you can sit comfortably.

name: Yassıtepe Höyüğü Karşılama Merkezi
text: Very attentive team here. Even though the mound is relatively new, the artifacts inside are really valuable. In about 20 years this place will probably be in a very different state.

name: Zuhal Müzik İzmir
address: Gazi Bulvarı Münir Birsel Plaza 95/A, D:14, 35210 Konak/İzmir
text: A music store with a huge variety and very knowledgeable staff.

name: Terramur Deniz Ürünleri Restoranı
address: Yenikent, İzmir Çeşme Cd. No:165, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text:
The setting is upscale, the clientele is a notch above average, but the prices aren't inflated. We'd say it's on par with a standard fish restaurant by Urla standards. We paid around 2,500 per person.

The food was generally tasty, and we didn't eat anything bad. Sardines, tarama, and herb sautee were our favorites. The armola cheese was a bit heavy; they could've warned us about that. The seafood variety is quite nice and different. The salt-baked fish looked great.

name: Urla Sayfiye Evi Restoran
text:
The atmosphere is truly incredible, the seaside is gorgeous, and it gives amazing views especially at sunset.
The food is quite good. The mezes are okay but the fish is really delicious. I'd recommend focusing on the fish rather than the mezes.
The service, however, is completely unworthy of a place like this. If we hadn't been persistent, the waiters wouldn't have even taken our orders. They don't pay proper attention. Everything we asked for came out wrong. Their heads are in the clouds.
If they fix the service, it'll be a perfect 10 restaurant. Worth going back for the great fish, but you need to go in with zero expectations about the service.

name: Tepekahve Çetin'in Yeri
address: Barbaros Urlu Köyü Mevki Eski, Merkez, İzmir-Çeşme Yolu, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: A no-frills, delicious spread-style breakfast spot with natural ingredients and no waste. A perfect 10. The owners are very attentive, the food is fresh. Everything was delicious except the sucuk. A place that delivers exactly what you'd expect. Prices are fair for the setting.

name: Sala VIP Cibeles
text:
Food was ok, nothing fancy and hot food but still options exist such as salad bar etc.
Good variety of wines and beers.

name: Pago de Carraovejas
address: Camino de Carraovejas, s/n, 47300 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Valladolid, Spain
text:
Thank you so much for your rating and review, Ozgur!

We hope to see you again soon!

name: Anagora Wine Zone
address: Calle de, C. Derecha Al Coso, 31, 47300 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain
text: They have some premium wines which you can taste with coravin, including Flor de Pingus and Vega Sicilia. Definitely worth visiting.
I do not know why this shows the plaza is closed, but it is open for 24 hours and no tickets etc needed.

name: Ágora de Protos
text:
Reservation can make sense as it was nearly fully booked when we were there.
Food is good. You can also drink every wine Protos have as a glass, including grand reservas.

name: Restaurante “Molino de Palacios”
address: Av. de la Constitucion, 16, 47300 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain
text:
They have really good old wines in the menu and good for the price.
The food was really great. Especially white beans and the meat was awesome. Really helpful staff.

name: Entrada-Camino Aalto Bodegas y Viñedos
address: Paraje Vallejo de Carril, s/n, 47360 Quintanilla de Arriba, Valladolid, Spain
text:
Tasting is for free, but need to make reservation as it is nearly fully booked.
They have 3 wines, 2 red and 1 white. Reds are good but they are too young to drink as the tour guide also told that. White was really good.

name: Urit Restaurant
text: It's a superb spot as a municipality-run place. Definitely somewhere you should go for fish and salads. The mezes are average but the fish flavors are superb. Perfect for sticking with fresh fish, greens, and yogurt-free/cheese-free vegetables like sea beans and artichokes.

name: HAMiYET ANNE PİDE SALONU MURAT USTANiN YERİ
text:
The setting is laid-back but really enjoyable.
The pides are original. The ones with entrecote and artichoke were superb. We had higher expectations for the shrimp and octopus ones. They weren't bad either, but the pesto sauce and cheese combo was a bit heavy. They could've added something lighter to balance it out.
The owners are great fun. Chatting with Hamiyet anne was lovely.

name: CHOP BBQ BURGER GÜZELBAHÇE
address: Kahramandere, Ata Cd. No:52 D:H, 35310 Güzelbahçe/İzmir
text: The burgers weren't bad, but the service was really slow. On Friday evening we waited almost an hour. Don't come too hungry :)

name: BosphoreLounge
text: The food is honestly mediocre. We went for breakfast and there was nothing breakfast-like. They threw out leftover meze from the night before. One of the most underwhelming lounges I've ever been to.

name: Devran Beach Restaurant
text:
There's probably nothing more absurd than having paid and limited showers at a place like this. You need to buy a token to use the shower. Total nonsense.

One of the most beautiful seas in the world, yet the beach couldn't be any dirtier. A complete disappointment.

It has no upside other than the sunbeds. Instead of staying here, go to the facilities inside Kapalımaraş that have tables and chairs without sunbeds; at least you'll have peace of mind.

name: Cava Wine Lounge
text: One of the rare places that sells Cypriot wine by the glass. The glass selection is limited though. For a place that calls itself a wine lounge, I honestly couldn't justify having so few options by the glass. But when we didn't like one of the glasses, they noticed and offered to swap it for another. That was a positive.

name: The Palm House
address: Laguna Apartments, Palm Beach, Magusa, North
text: Wine selection is a bit limited. Nice view though. Food is good but service is a bit slow. Staff are friendly and pleasant. A good spot to spend a long time enjoying the view and the weather.

name: Castello Restaurant & Bar
text:
The food is great, portions are generous, prices are cheap compared to Turkey. We had lamb chops and chicken shish. Each main comes with 5 mezes + halloumi + salad for free. What more could you ask for.

The staff are very attentive. There are at least 20-30 wine-by-the-glass options, which was quite surprising.

name: Publik Cafe & Bar
text: A nice cafe. They have 3-4 different wine-by-the-glass options. Self-service. Enjoyable.

name: La Regina Veneziana Guest House
text:
The rooms are beautifully designed, spacious, and new. Just a slight sound insulation issue since the doors etc. are old, but if they fixed that it'd be perfect.

The owner Tolga bey is very friendly and extremely attentive. Despite our last-minute inability to come and not having a free cancellation, they let us use our booking later. When a taxi driver ditched us on the way back, they even drove us to the station. Much appreciated.

Breakfast is superb, and location-wise it's really enjoyable.

name: Lime Alaçatı - İstinyePark İzmir
address: Bahçelerarası Mah. Şehit Binbaşı Ali Resmi Tufan Sok, Pazaryeri Katı, D:1B11, İstinyePark AVM No: 3, 35330 Balçova/İzmir
text: Food is really good and high quality, lots of options in the healthy concept, relaxed atmosphere, and the staff are very attentive.

name: Ciğerci Sinan
text: Portions are big, food is good. No home delivery. There's a non-smoking area issue, but other than that the food is solid.

name: Gazi Çorba & Köfte
text: Food is really good, prices are reasonable, portions are big. Perfect for home-style meals and a quick bite.

name: Eniştem Lokantası
address: İstiklal Mahallesi Muğla Bulvarı No:56/A Aydın Göz Hastanesi 500m Aşağısı, 09100 Efeler/Aydın
text: Standard home-cooked meals, unfortunately nothing really stood out for us.

name: Yörük Efe Kasap & Restaurant
address: Sığacık Mahallesi Eski Sığacık Yolu Caddesi, Sokak No:8/1, 35460 Seferihisar/İzmir
text: Food is mediocre, the place is dusty and smoky, and in the summer heat it's a terrible choice. Forget AC, they don't even have fans. Don't be fooled by the rating.

name: Pegasus
text:
Rooms are a little bit old, but not disturbing.
Owners are really nice and helpful.
Has really good location.
Good for the price.

name: Pergamonto Restaurant
text: One of the few places that has a good breakfast option. It has both traditional and new generation options.

name: P2 Cafe
text: Good for a late night drink, they have good wine options, including glass ones.

name: Remataki
text:
Their atmosphere is nice, but the food was a little bit disappointment. Little portions and regular flavors. Do not have big expectations.
Sunbeds have prices.

name: Ευ Ζην
text: Sunbeds are for free, however their menu is so limited as they do not have any seafood etc.

name: Chatziioannou
text:
Really good wines. Especially really enjoyed Jackal.
Good owners. Tasting is a little bit expensive, but worth for the quality.
Do not have expectations for the tasting area.

name: Samos Wine Museum
text: It is good as museum, however wines are in the form of table wines. So, no expectations for wines would be better for you.

name: Joy food&coffee
text: Nice view, good menu and food. But the service is really really slow.

name: To Kainourgio Restaurant
text: Sea is really nice. Good for hangout all day. Family business. Sunbeds had price. Service is slow.

name: Fong Seng Leilai Lembrancas
address: Edificio Kam Wa, 10號 MacaoR. de São Paulo, Macao
text: If you're passing through the area, don't forget to say hi to Mustafa! Greetings to him.

name: Bloom
address: Edificio Comercial Rodrigues, Av. da Praia Grande, Macao
text: Really interesting ice cream place. I really enjoyed vanilla-truffle and earl grey-chocolate. They might not be good for all, but it is still good to have that kind of interesting stuff.

name: İkidenizarasi Winery
text:
Since it's a newly opened winery, we honestly went with low expectations, but it was quite the opposite; they exceeded them by far. Their white wines especially are very successful; we loved the Bornova Misket and Chardonnay. The reds aren't full-bodied but they're easy-drinking wines.

The restaurant is excellent. We got oyster mushrooms and risotto balls as appetizers and loved both. The olive tapenade they brought as a complimentary starter was also great. They won us over with their food, and we're definitely going back for the restaurant :)

Note from 2025: The chef had changed at the restaurant but has changed again. In its current state, it's back to its former performance.

name: ÇAKIR Winery & Vineyards/ ÇAKIR Şarapçılık & Bağcılık
text:
2022: The place has a nice ambiance, we didn't eat but did a wine tasting. The presentation and service were good. Their Chardonnay wines are truly incredible. We didn't quite find what we expected from their other wines relative to the price range, but still worth a try :)

Edit from 2025: Make sure to call before you go and make a reservation. If you're going for a tasting, specifically mention that and ask about availability. Otherwise you might show up and be told the tasting is in 2 hours.

name: USCA Winery
text:
Nice wines. Especially the Claret and Sonnet 23-76 are a must-try.

Edit from 2025: They've stopped allowing children, though they used to. Heads up for those who weren't aware.

name: Çiy Restaurant & Konukevi
text:
Unfortunately we didn't find what we expected from the restaurant. Prices have skyrocketed, and the food absolutely doesn't justify them. We're used to these prices at chef's restaurants, but naturally we'd also expect quite different plates in return. The sea bass wasn't much different from what you'd get at a regular fish restaurant, just dressed up with tomato sauce. It's maybe 200 grams at most. Charging 3,700 TL for a plate like that is completely absurd.

The ambiance is quite nice, but the food fell below our expectations.

name: Yedi Toprak Sofrası
address: Kuşçular, Kuşcular Cd. No:81 D:1B, 35435 Urla/İzmir
text:
The setting is quite nice and the service staff are good. The menu is quite original. Different flavors have been interpreted beautifully. The Loznik, Hummus, and Sultan's Delight were really excellent.

The prices aren't super affordable but the taste definitely justifies them.

The fact that all the wines on the menu are from local grape selections also deserves extra credit.

name: Ciro Urla
text: Probably because they just opened, the prices are very reasonable right now. The food is delicious. A true value-for-money spot. Once they get their liquor license and add wine to the menu, it'll be even better. Hope they don't mess up this pricing.

name: Kargıcak Koyu Dalyan
address: Kargıcak Koyu, Gökbel, Kargıcak Sokak No:41, 48600 Ortaca/Muğla
text: Food and drinks are quite high quality, service and atmosphere are satisfying. The sea is great. If only the road there were better, it'd be flawless :)

name: Kaplumbağa Hastanesi
text: You guys are truly amazing. This country keeps going thanks to wonderful people like you. I got so emotional visiting. Thank you for being there, beautiful people.

name: Iztuzu Beach Cafe
text: A pretty comfortable spot to hang out at Iztuzu.

name: La Boheme Dalyan
text: We went just for drinks in the evening. The menu is very extensive; they have a wine selection you wouldn't even find in city centers. There's a view of the rock tombs. The place is beautifully designed. Service was flawless.

name: River House Restaurant
text: Food was okay-level, and it got a bit tiring going with a big group. Service was as fast as it could be though. Nice location atmosphere-wise.

name: Yener's Place
text:
The food came quite late. Despite arriving early and sitting down, we waited almost an hour for our food. The fact that we weren't informed about this beforehand wore the group out a bit.
The food quality was generally good. Portions are filling. Prices are okay for a summer spot.
The setting is nice but there's a serious mosquito problem.

name: Bc Spa Hotel
address: Dalyan Mah. Şehit Adil Büyükcengiz Sok. No:7, 48600 Ortaca/Muğla
text: The location is quite nice. The rooms are a bit old but both functional and spacious. Breakfast is pretty standard and ends early. Atmosphere-wise it's a quite nice hotel.

name: Dac Restaurant
address: Altintas mahallesi, Gazi Bulvari no:175, 48770 Dalaman/Muğla
text: An absolutely incredible place. I wish we could replicate this everywhere in Turkey. Food is really good, prices are fair, and the staff work their hearts out. If you go a bit earlier, you can get a table without waiting in line.

name: Kup Sarapcilik
address: Mahmutgazi Köyü Taşbağları Mevki, No:205, 20700 Çal/Denizli
text: The new tasting areas are very stylish. No food options but they have snacks like cheese boards. The historic building and old winery section (museum) are beautiful. A must-visit.

name: Madame Prokopp
address: Alsancak, Muzaffer İzgü Sk. No: 2, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text: The setting is great. The food is tasty. The service didn't falter. We went for a tasting event and were quite satisfied.

name: Bademler Sanat Köyü
text: Nothing to complain about regarding the atmosphere and ambiance; it's really enjoyable during concerts, but their menu is an absolute disaster. They supposedly have frankfurter sausage on the menu, but they're selling the cheapest supermarket chicken sausage as frankfurter. Their nachos? The chips are barely a step up from regular corn chips. We can safely say we had the worst food we've eaten in Urla in a long time. The restaurant itself deserves 1 star, but I'm giving 3 purely because of the great setting and ambiance. I absolutely do not recommend eating here.

name: Fuego Urla
address: M. Fevzi Çakmak, 4153. Sk. No:16, 35445 Urla/İzmir
text:
A fun whisky bar. The view is beautiful, the atmosphere is very enjoyable, and they have plenty of whisky options. The waiter could use some improvement on whisky knowledge though; he doesn't know much about it :)
It's a shame there's no kitchen; some small snacks would've been nice.

name: Tare Suites Urla
text: A pretty nice spot for swimming. The new design turned out really well. Prices are good and the food is tasty too.

name: Island Vintage Coffee Odaiba
address: Japan, 〒135-0091 Tokyo, Minato City, Daiba, 1 Chome−6−1 デックス東京ビーチシーサイドモール ３F
text: Lousy breakfast, rude staff, and undercooked bacon. Not worth it.

name: teamLab Planets TOKYO DMM
address: 6 Chome-1-16 Toyosu, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0061, Japan
text: If you have a kid, then it would be enjoyable. Otherwise, no need to go. Mostly a tourist trap.

name: Hard Rock Cafe Tokyo Roppongi
address: 5 Chome-4-20 Roppongi, Minato City, Tokyo 106-0032, Japan
text: Just as great as any other Hard Rock. Top-notch experience.

name: ROAR COFFEE Tokyo Ginza
address: Japan, 〒104-0061 Tokyo, Chuo City, Ginza, 3 Chome−12−５第二 宏和ビル 1F
text: Nice banana bread, ice cream, and coffee.

name: Wine Shop Ginza - Vinos & Yamazaki
address: 6 Chome-2-3 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0061, Japan
text: They have 8 wine fridges, offer 5 wines by the glass, and an unlimited bottle selection. They also sell bottles on-site, fully stocked. An amazing spot. They have both Japanese and international wines, and also sake.

name: TsuruTonTan UDON NOODLE Brasserie GINZA
address: Japan, 〒104-0061 Tokyo, Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−2−1 GinzaNovo10階
text: The udon was top-notch, and the sushi and tempura were also really good.

name: Coco Nemaru Ginza
address: Japan, 〒104-0061 Tokyo, Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−3−18 ＬａＬａ Ｇｒａｎｄｅ GＩＮＺＡ １階
text: Incredible wagyu, the food was genuinely superb. I only wish they had a more varied and better wine list for such quality meat and restaurant.

name: Fish Market Tsukiji Outer Market
address: Japan, 〒104-0045 Tokyo, Chuo City, Tsukiji, 4 Chome および６丁目一部
text: Absolutely amazing seafood. We had king crab legs for 5,000 yen, giant prawns... a paradise for seafood lovers.

name: Hotel Villa Fontaine Grand Shiodome
address: Japan, 〒105-0021 Tokyo, Minato City, Higashishinbashi, 1 Chome−9−2 内
text: Metro station right at the entrance. Super comfortable hotel, and we loved the rooms. Excellent choice overall.

name: Gonpachi Nishi-Azabu
address: 1 Chome-13-11 Nishiazabu, Minato City, Tokyo 106-0031, Japan
text: The famous Kill Bill restaurant. Great vibe and food. Clearly designed for tourists, but they did a great job. The chicken skewers and the seaweed salad with mussels were excellent, and the wine selection was also good.

name: ASAKUSA SUMO CLUB
address: 2 Chome-10-12 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
text: A place that offers both a meal and a show. A bit pricey, but it's all-you-can-eat and the sumo performance is quite entertaining. A must for sumo fans.

name: Suke6 Diner
address: Japan, 〒111-0033 Tokyo, Taito City, Hanakawado, 1 Chome−11−1 あゆみビル
text: One of the best breakfasts we had in Japan. It usually says there's a queue and shows no availability on TableCheck, but we only waited 5 minutes. Going a bit later might help avoid the crowd.

name: WINE SHOP CornerBar BLANCO asakusa ワインショップ コーナーバー ブランコ 浅草
address: Japan, 〒111-0035 Tokyo, Taito City, Nishiasakusa, 2 Chome−17−5 ラシクラス西浅草 １階
text: They had four Japanese wines and a total of eight by-the-glass options. The owners were very attentive, and the atmosphere was nice.

name: Shochikuen Cafe
address: 2 Chome-7-6 Nishiasakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0035, Japan
text: A lovely vegan cafe. The desserts and coffee were excellent.

name: Pepper Parlor
address: Japan, 〒150-0043 Tokyo, Shibuya, Dogenzaka, 1 Chome−2−3 東急プラザ渋谷 5F
text: A robot cafe, fun for the memes, but the robots are super dumb. We're waiting for an AI update.

name: PREMIER MAI
address: Japan, 〒151-0063 Tokyo, Shibuya, Tomigaya, 1 Chome−6−10 代々木公園ビル 2F
text: Like a fine dining breakfast spot but not too expensive. The yellow omelette and cheese toast were great, and the coffee was high quality. There's no sign outside, so you have to ask around to find it :)

name: Biople ルミネエスト新宿店
address: Japan, 〒160-0022 Tokyo, Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−1 ルミネエスト新宿 B2F
text: We really liked the range and the quality of the products.

name: Trattoria Pizzeria Bar FAVETTA
address: Japan, 〒160-0021 Tokyo, Shinjuku City, Kabukicho, 1 Chome−23−14 第1メトロビル 1階
text: Wine options were practically nonexistent, which is shocking for an Italian restaurant. We had mushroom pizza and chicken, and it was just okay. Nothing too good, but you can go for it if you do not have any options.

name: Dog Cafe Rio in SHINJUKU
address: 1 Chome-15-7 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
text: The smell inside was terrible. I was completely put off by the concept. Totally unnecessary and not worth visiting at all.

name: Komeda's Coffee
address: 66 Fukakusa Inarionmaecho, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto, 612-0881, Japan
text: The smell from the smoking room leaks out and is disgusting. The coffee is okay, the desserts are so-so, just a standard spot. No alternative milk options.

name: kyocafe chacha
address: Japan, 〒616-0016 Kyoto, Nishikyo Ward, Arashiyama Kamikaidocho, ４８
text: One of the few places in the Arashiyama area where you can have breakfast. Child-friendly, with a dedicated eating area and a changing station for kids. The staff are very attentive.

name: musubi sweets factory
address: 51 Arashiyama Nakaoshitacho, Nishikyo Ward, Kyoto, 616-0004, Japan
text: A cozy place with tasty gluten-free and vegan snacks, not too sweet, and quite enjoyable.

name: BnA Alter Museum Cafe/Lounge
text: Nice breakfast, baby-friendly with an elevator, has eggs, etc., for breakfast. If you're craving a Western-style breakfast, you can go here.
The crème brûlée donut was really good.

name: Maccha House
address: 3 Chome-337 Kiyomizu, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0862, Japan
text: A high-quality place, but eating and drinking matcha is quite challenging :) The Uji matcha was pretty good.

name: East End Gion
address: Japan, 〒605-0083 Kyoto, Higashiyama Ward, Hashimotocho, 382 モンシェリビル １F
text: A tiny, cozy sushi spot with only six seats, tucked away in a side street. We were very happy with everything we tried. All the sushi was great, especially the shrimp tempura sushi, which was our favorite. We found the classic style here that we couldn't find elsewhere.

name: Onimaru
text: It was excellent, a must-try. The shrimp and salmon-avocado ones were especially good. They have their own dining area upstairs, but most people just get takeaway, so the upstairs is quite comfortable.

name: Kobe Beef Kisshokichi - Kyoto Main Store
address: Japan, 〒604-8001 Kyoto, Nakagyo Ward, Kamiosakacho, 521 京都エンパイヤビル 8階
text:
The prices are very high, but that's probably because Kobe beef is always expensive. The concept is great; they cook it in front of you. The staff really struggle with English, which is surprising for such a touristy spot. Prices are high but normal for Kobe beef. The red wine menu was terrible, very disappointing for a meat-focused restaurant.

All the places smell food, which is a little bit disgusting. The ventilation was poor.

name: Libero
address: Japan, 〒604-8086 Kyoto, Nakagyo Ward, Maruyacho, 333 エレメントビル
text: Mainly American wines, a small place with just six glass options are available, also Coravin is available for other bottles but a little bit expensive. The owner is very attentive. A great spot for those who want to do some wine tasting.

name: annee
address: Japan, 〒604-8202 Kyoto, Nakagyo Ward, Tsukinukecho, 139 プリモフィオーレ室町 1F
text: The best breakfast we've had in the entire Far East so far. The madame croque and quiche were excellent. For takeaway, the shrimp tartine and quiche were also superb.

name: Kushiyaki Manten Rokkakuhen
text: Chicken gizzard is really hard to eat if you are not familiar with. Other than that, other chickens are really good, really like the corn and edamame, grilled rice and dumplings.

name: TOKYU STAY Kyoto Sanjo-Karasuma
text: The rooms felt a bit small, but they were functional. The beds are huge. The washing machine works well. The staff are attentive. There is coffee and water available in the lobby. The location is excellent. Overall, we were very satisfied.

name: Flames
address: Hong Kong, Hung Hom, Shop G10B, Fashion World Site 2 - Cherry Mansions, Whampoa Garden 9 Shung King Street
text:
Food is nice but you can order some food only as a set of menu which includes a soft drink. Even if you do not want it, you cannot order the food only.

No restrooms inside.

Risotto was ok, beef was a little bit rough.

name: Scarlett Café & Wine Bar
address: New Austin Commercial Centre, 2 Austin Ave, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong
text:
Food is great. Salmon tartar, pike fish, octopus. We really enjoyed.
I cannot say the same for the wine. Really lack of glass options, which is not great for a wine bar.

name: Above & Beyond
address: 28/F, 17 Science Museum Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong
text:
Really good wine menu.
Dinner is a little bit different. Definitely need to get familiar within chinese food before. Some of them are pretentious. Didn't enjoy passion fruit soup.
Original food but may not be suitable for all.

name: noc (Whampoa)
address: Shop G42, G/F, Whampoa Garden Site 9 Block 1, Tak Fung St, Hung Hom, Hong Kong
text: Good coffee, perfect breakfast!

name: Resbyne Restaurant
address: G/F, 4 Pound Ln, Sheung Wan, Hong Kong
text: Wagyu, pasta, orange salad, garlic bread was nice. Selection of wine was not so good.

name: Harbour Grand Kowloon
address: Whampoa Garden, 20 Tak Fung St, Hung Hom, Hong Kong
text: Great location and good view. Rooms were nice. Breakfast was meh. If you are staying above 20th floor, you need to change the elevators, which is a little bit long.

name: O'Thai
address: One Harbourfront, 18 Tak Fung Street, Whampoa Garden, Hung Hom, Hong Kong
text: Really good atmosphere and food. We mostly ate on tapas. Lobster sandwich, calamari and fish taco was the best.

name: Plaza Premium Lounge (Gate 1, East Hall)
address: Hong Kong International Airport, Near Gate 1, Departures, Lantau Island, Hong Kong
text: There is no western breakfast at all :)

name: Mystery Foods Gluten-Free Atelier Alsancak
address: Kültür mahallesi, Alsancak, 1375. Sk. No:6 D:C, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text: The menu is generally diverse, a nice restaurant for those who prefer gluten-free. Portions are just a bit small, but the flavors are good. The only downside is it's way too hot to go in the summer since they weren't running the AC.

name: Ceviz Garden Urla
address: Naipli, Mustafa Kemal Paşa Cd. no:149/a, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: This venue where we occasionally hold our dance parties in Urla is quite spacious as a place, and the food is a total bang for your buck. We've been very happy on every visit so far.

name: Atilla Heilbronn: Fine Dining
text:
First off, the location on the map is wrong. Just navigate directly to Narimor Hotel.

This was one of the most impressive meals I've had in a long time. As a couple who normally doesn't enjoy the tasting-menu concept, I can say we were blown away by Atilla Bey's dishes. It's not easy to find plates this meticulously crafted with every detail thought through just anywhere. Along with Vino Locale in Urla, I'd say it's become one of our favorite restaurants. The quality of the wines served as pairings could perhaps be bumped up a notch, maybe a dedicated pairing study for the dishes. That was the only shortcoming we found. Beyond that, there's not a single bad thing to say about the food. Hoping to visit again as soon as possible!

name: Azur Yeniköy
text:
The setting is truly spectacular, beautifully designed with an incredible Bosphorus view. There were some small service hiccups. We asked for a salad without tomatoes and it came with tomatoes. When we sent it back, it came back clearly picked-through with lots of tomato remnants etc. That was unbecoming of a restaurant at this level.
The food quality is quite good. We were generally happy with what we ate. The caviar plate had an unnecessary price markup on it, and we paid almost as much for that as for everything else we ate. At a place where you order without a menu, they really need to mention when something is extremely expensive.
Apart from that, the wine menu looks quite extensive but especially the whites are stuck on old vintages. 2022 whites are already well past their prime, and while I think this is about clearing old stock, I can't accept that from a place on the Michelin recommendation list.

name: Hilton Istanbul Maslak
text: The location and rooms make it a quite nice hotel, but they really need to update their minibars etc. It's 2025, and a quality hotel like Hilton is stocking the bottom-tier Kavaklıdere wines in the minibar. This menu that hasn't changed since 1995 needs to go. It's unworthy of Hilton.

name: Balmumu Dükkan Lokanta
address: Kültür Mah, Alsancak, 1388. Sk. No: 5 D:C, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text: Everything we ate was really delicious, and there are especially great and healthy options for vegetable-focused eating. You can also find items unique to this place that you won't find elsewhere, like the small eggplants. A nice stop for healthy food.

name: Kaptanoğlu Restoran
text:
A huge plus that there's no smoking inside during winter; most places don't give us that luxury anymore.
The fish and seafood are quite tasty. The smoked mussels were our favorite.

name: Le Deck Restaurant
address: Rte de la Corniche 4, 1070 Puidoux, Switzerland
text:
Really nice view of lake. Service and atmosphere is just great.

Food was also good. Really like the salads, caviar table and the fish they served. Definitely recommend here.

name: Gaillard et Fils SA
text:
Good view of the lake.
Wine tasting menu is good as well as the food.

name: Les 11 Terres
address: Rle du Petit Crêt 2, 1098 Epesses, Switzerland
text: Good tasting menu/selection of wines and pairing food.

name: Le Relais de l'Entrecôte
text: Really delicious food, fixed menu and it is awesome. Plus the portions are really big.

name: Crystal Lounge by Aspire, Geneva Airport
address: Rte de l'Aéroport 21, 1215 Le Grand-Saconnex, Switzerland
text: Not worth to visit, there is literally little food options in here.

name: Globe of Science and Innovation
text: Awesome place to visit, definitely worth visiting if you are into science.

name: Deniz Yıldızı Kokoreç’çi Özcan
text: The kokoreç is tasty, went nicely with a beer. It's good, not quite at the "you absolutely must go" level, but if you live in Urla and fancy having kokoreç by the seaside, it's a spot you can try. Prices are reasonable.

name: Casalyma Ayvalık
text:
+ The owners and staff are incredibly warm people, truly wonderful. They took care of everything, much appreciated
+ Breakfast was quite good
+ It's in a great location, the seaside is very enjoyable
+ If you get the rooms with a view, the scenery is really beautiful

- Unfortunately the hotel is an old building. Sound insulation is zero. It felt like we were sharing a room with the neighbors. Sounds from 4 rooms down could be heard in ours. If you're a light sleeper, you could have a rough night.
- Also due to the insulation issue, cigarette smoke from other rooms can drift into yours. If you're sensitive about that, it could bother you.
- Despite the hotel being old, the rooms have been renovated, but there are some design flaws. For instance, in our room the toilet and sink were crammed so close together that it's impossible to sit straight on the toilet. You can only sit sideways, which unfortunately ruins the comfort.

name: Carne RESTAURANT
address: Çanakkale-Bursa Karayolu 4. Km 2. sokak NO:2/2 - Merkez, 17100 Çanakkale Merkez/Çanakkale
text:
Food is very reasonably priced for the quality, the wine menu is amazing, and the service is really good.
The liver was excellent, the red pepper sauce they brought with it was great too, and the mini corn was unforgettable.

name: Μπουγάτσα Ρεκόρ
text: Perfect food! Really liked the quality. Spinach, cheese and meat options all were awesome. Really good for the breakfast.

name: Винарна "Четири сезона"
address: Blagoevgrad Center, ul. "Todor Alexandrov" 23, 2700 Blagoevgrad, Bulgaria
text:
Food is ok but I cannot say the same for the wine. They are actually a wine house but they only have 1 option as a glass for all kind of wines (red, rose, white) which are regular table wines. So as a wine bar, I would not find it acceptable.

Design seems good at first place but some places have a little bit absurdity.

name: Boutique Apartments Blagoevgrad
address: Blagoevgrad Center, ul. "Todor Alexandrov" 29, 2700 Blagoevgrad, Bulgaria
text: Really nice staff. Rooms are clean, location is very good-only problem is parking. Nice sound isolation. Very good for the price.

name: Шапковатъ кръчмъ
text: View and atmosphere is really nice, but the service is lacking at the restaurant. Still, nice area to look to the view.

name: OENOGENESIS S.A.
address: Επί επαρχιακής οδού Δοξάτο-Αδριανή, Adriani 661 00, Greece
text: Really good winery. They have a unique approach to the wines. Mostly they sell vintage/aged wines which are unusual. Their prices are really good comparing to the performance. Definitely worth visiting.

name: Potina Restaurant
text: Pizzas are excellent and the tenderloin was really good too. We didn't like it when we came before, but the switch from a fixed menu to à la carte was a great move.

name: Köfteci Ramiz
text: Breakfast is mediocre.

name: De Vooruitgang
text: Perfect atmosphere, great drinks!!

name: VERONA 63
text: Location is really good, but the food was a little bit touristic.

name: Hard Rock Cafe Amsterdam
text: Good atmosphere, food and service as always!

name: IJs- en wafelhuis Volendam
text: We didn't like the quality of the ice creams

name: Pasta Kodu Plus, İskele
address: İskele, Yorgo Seferis Sk. No:5, 35441 Urla/İzmir
text: Breakfast is quite delicious, nice atmosphere, food comes out fast. The breakfast spot İskele needed.

name: Hilton Bursa Convention Center & Spa
text: Rooms are quite nice and spacious, service is great. Location is spot on. Staying here might be better than the Hampton; the price difference is small but there seems to be a quality gap. We were happy with everything.

name: İskender Efendi Konağı
address: Soğanlı, Soğanlı Botanik Parkı No:1, 16140 Osmangazi̇/Bursa
text: Atmosphere is great, food is quite high quality, the place is nicely designed and modernized. Prices aren't expensive for the quality either.

name: Yorgo Social Urla
text:
Supposedly fine dining but they don't have a wine menu. Not only that, there are only 2 glass/bottle options. On the menu they brought, the prices were crossed out with new updated prices written in etc.
We invest millions building fancy venues but can't be bothered to print a proper menu. We open a fine dining restaurant but have no options beyond a few table wines. We still have a very long way to go unfortunately.

name: Noon Good Food
text: Their breakfasts were really enjoyable. The cashew cheeses are super delicious. All the desserts we took home were also great. You can confidently order anything.

name: Seyhan Et
address: Yelaltı, Nur Dikmen Cd. No:20, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: Food and atmosphere are great, the wine menu is quite nice. Everything we had was really enjoyable.

name: Şaşula Çerez & Kahve
address: Yenikent, Nur Dikmen Cd. No:5/B, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: Their coffees are quite delicious. Also great to buy and enjoy at home.

name: Ops İskele
text: The food is quite tasty. The sea salt and espresso tarts were really good. Lots of gluten-free and healthy options available. The only thing was their crackers didn't really appeal to us.

name: Cumhuriyet Fırını
address: İskele, İskele Cd. No:16 D:A, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: They have really nice and healthy products. Both the sweets and the gluten-free items are quite delicious.

name: Cafe Merci
text:
Usually empty, has a nice view from the front section. Teas/coffees are good. Self-service.

Their honey cakes are incredibly delicious. They usually come in on Saturday and Sunday.

name: L'uva Restaurant
text:
Their wine-by-the-glass menu is quite extensive and they serve good wines. The ambiance is quite elegant.

The food is quite good, the meat dishes were really enjoyable. Steak and ossobuco can be eaten with total confidence.

name: Gula Urla
address: kuşçular mahallesi kuşçular caddesi kuşçular :46, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text:
The place is really beautiful atmosphere-wise. Both the interior and the garden are quite enjoyable. The menu looks like a lot of care went into it, but unfortunately the flavors didn't quite hit the mark for us. An arancini ball, a common appetizer at restaurants like this, had rice that was somehow both mushy and dry at the same time. The calamari etc. were so-so. The fish we had was good, but from a place with a Michelin recommendation, we expected much more than just good fish.

The service was quite poor. Despite going on a weekday, our questions and requests about the food always got lost. We constantly had to remind them 2, even 3 times. Clearly the number of servers is insufficient. If it's this chaotic on a weekday, I can't imagine what weekends must be like.

name: Melo Cheesecakes & Cookies
text:
We tried their 2 most famous desserts and they weren't bad, but we didn't find them as good as they're hyped up to be. Maybe we went with too high expectations.

The indoor area is very small, with very little seating for eating in.

name: Erçin Daysal
text: Incredibly delicious, fresh meats. A butcher shop where you can shop with total peace of mind.

name: Mado Swissotel Mado
text: Breakfast isn't very impressive. We ordered an omelet that came overcooked, didn't taste great, and despite the place being empty, it took forever to arrive.

name: Pietra Social House
text: Amazing place. The owners are attentive about everything, and whenever the smallest issue comes up, they make sure to address and fix it. A beloved spot in our Urla where you can confidently go for any kind of gathering with friends, and the live music is a nice touch too.

name: Cleto's Ristorante Italiano
address: 150 Evler Mah. Atatürk Bul. Kırlangıç Yaşam Merkezi, 10400 Ayvalık/Balıkesir
text:
An Italian restaurant where the red wines are served ice cold. One of the wines on their by-the-glass menu was clearly corked, and when we pointed it out, they said they knew that's how the wine was. No comment :)

Half the items on their menu are unavailable due to seasonal reasons. If it were 1-2 items, fine, but if half the menu is going to be missing, then they either need to note which months items are available, or create a seasonal menu. In its current state, it's quite hard to even place an order.

The food quality isn't bad. We liked the bolognese pasta, and their pizzas are quite fresh. Just if you're not someone who loves too much tomato sauce, it might feel excessive. The sauce was a bit raw. The pizza crust edges were actually at an edible consistency, but ours came a bit too burnt.

name: Cunda Despot Evi
address: Cunda Adası, Mithatpaşa mh. Mektep Sk. N:2/2, 10400 Ayvalık/Balıkesir
text:
A beautifully designed hotel with superb attention to detail in the rooms. From the slippers to the hair dryer, the highest quality choices I've ever seen in a hotel. They also offer a complimentary glass of wine.

There's parking at the entrance. The sea view is quite enjoyable.

The breakfast included at Kosebasi wasn't quite satisfying enough. From a hotel with this concept, we'd expect a much better breakfast.

name: Novavera
text:
Our favorite olive oil shop in the area. Their olives are quite good too. If you shop at their retail store here, there's a 20% discount.
We also really liked their bergamot and spicy olive oils.

name: Salepcioğlu Helvaları
text: Incredibly delicious and fresh halva. So good that even someone who doesn't like halva could enjoy it.

name: Altin Kepce Bergama Meatball
address: Turabey, Hacı Yamak Sk. No:1, 35700 Bergama/İzmir
text: The food tastes decent, but it's your standard meatball-and-eggplant deal, nothing extra special. We weren't fans of the chickpea börek.

name: Kasap Ahmetin Yeri
address: Alparslan Türkeş Bulvarı Dört Yol Mevkii Ortaklar, 09330 Germencik/Aydın
text: Nice shish kebabs, good meat quality, good mezes, prices aren't over the top.

name: EV URLA RESTORAN
text:
Incredible flavor. The meat quality is absolutely outstanding. The burger's taste lingered on our palates, juicy and so good. We really liked the sauced tenderloin, entrecote, and ribs. For desserts, the chocolate tart was superb, like a Snickers cake. The zucchini salad was also great.

The tasting menu is pricey for the portion sizes. Instead of going with the tasting, ordering the same items as a shared platter works out cheaper.

name: Swissôtel Büyük Efes İzmir
address: Alsancak, Gazi Osman Paşa Blv. No:1, 35210 Konak/İzmir
text: Rooms are great, high quality and comfortable in every way. Reception and room service are unfortunately very slow though. The food quality at their harvest festival event this year was also disappointingly low.

name: Reyhan pastanesi - Alsancak
address: Kültür, Dr. Mustafa Enver Bey Cd. No:24, 35250 Konak/İzmir
text: Their raspberry cake and the new Dubai chocolate they just launched are amazing.

name: Hangout PSM
address: Metro Durağı, Kazımdirik, Üniversite Cd. No: 92, 35100 Bornova/İzmir
text: Restrooms aren't very clean, the staff are a bit uncoordinated. We were suffocating from the lack of ventilation during the 2-day festival.

name: 120 Urla
text: The atmosphere is beautiful, the food is genuinely top quality. We hadn't had such a good croissant in Turkey in a long time. Prices aren't over the top for the quality of the food. We loved it, definitely going back.

name: Holy Burek
text: Delicious borek's and ayran!

name: M'arden Dubrovnik
text: Place is just great. Lovely atmosphere and different selection of wines. Good alternative wine tasting menus.

name: Stara Loza
text: Went for breakfast and it was nice. Omelette and oatmeal was good.

name: Barba
text: Best seafood in town. Seafood platter 2 was just great. Amazing octopus burger, great fried squids.

name: Gianni
address: Ul. kneza Damjana Jude bb, 20000, Dubrovnik, Croatia
text: Best ice creams in town, as well as desserts. Black lotus and brownie was just great.

name: Wine Bar Škar Dubrovnik Old Town
text: They only sell their own wines. Service was really good, but I cannot say same for the wines. If you like natural kind of wines, then you can go. But if you are not, I can say you can pass this place.

name: Hard Rock Cafe Dubrovnik
text: Food and atmosphere was good as always globally, however cannot say the service for this one. Waiter confused our orders all the time and was a little bit rude.

name: Mama's Pot Tavern
text: Went for breakfast, omelette was ok, avocado toast was a disaster. It had barely avocado. Croissant is not croissant at all.

name: Dubravka 1836 Restaurant & Cafe
text:
Atmosphere is really nice, but I cannot say the same for the food. Ordered a seafood platter for 35 EUR, thinking as the portion would be big. It was neither big nor delicious.

They have a good wine menu but it seems that the wines are not hold on good circumstances because most of their aromas are gone.

name: Peppino’s Gelato Factory
text: It was good, but you can find better ones in the old town :)

name: Wine Museum Dubrovnik
text: A perfect museum if you are into wine. Good historical information, perfect tasting menus. They also have a shop, which has better prices comparing to the producer's.

name: Konoba Captain
text: Went for breakfast, omelette was ok.

name: Bakus Wine Bar
text: Their design is really good, but cannot say the same for the wines. The first wine that I tried was damaged. Bartender claimed it is normal. If the owner would not be in there, we were supposed to pay for a damaged wine.

name: D'VINO WINE BAR DUBROVNIK
text:
Best wine bar in Dubrovnik. If you want to go with flight tasting, you need to go before 9 PM.

They have a really good variety of glass wines. They also have changing menus for all different months. Really old vintage glasses are also available.

name: House Tereza
text: Perfect location, good sound isolation, clean and wide rooms.

name: Dubrovnik Airport Adriatic Business Lounge
text: We entered for breakfast. A lot of options for take it with you such as fruit (banana) or sandwiches. Liked the quality. Not too crowded.

name: Primeclass Lounge Adnan Menderes Havalimanı
address: Dokuz Eylül, Adnan Menderes Havaalanı, 35410 Gaziemir/İzmir
text: Much better designed as a space compared to the international terminal. Food is mediocre. Service is good.

name: iGA Lounge
text:
The entrance is unbelievably crowded. At least a 20-minute wait guaranteed. The congestion is insane.

Inside is just as packed. The space is clearly not enough. They need to allocate a much larger area.

name: Melo Cheesecakes&Cookies
text: The tiramisu was good, the banana pudding was decent too. We liked the cheesecakes as well. Overall the dessert quality is nice, but everything was a bit too sweet. The style leans more toward traditional desserts.

name: SushiCo
address: Hacı İsa, Necati Cumalı Cd. No:13, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: The service and atmosphere are nice, but the sushi quality was mediocre. They were a bit dry and bland.

name: Roku Sushi Omakase
text:
Both the hot appetizers and the sushi are really delicious. Prices are a bit steep but the quality justifies it.

The corn tempura and spicy sushi were outstanding.

name: Euphoria Hotel Batumi
address: 1 Lech and Maria Kaczynski St, Batumi, Georgia
text: Good rooms but if you are staying at first floor you cannot open the windows because it directly faces to the lower roof and this roof is full of cigar butts. Disgusting.

name: Ustabaş Lokantası
address: Merkez Kuledibi, Dumlupınar Cd. no:52F, 08600 Hopa/Artvin
text: It was the worst cag kebab I've ever had in my life. Half the food was burnt to charcoal and the other half was undercooked.

name: KumsalKaraokeLiveMusic
address: Musazade, Şht. Ast Mehmet Altanlar Cad No : 3B, 08200 Arhavi/Artvin
text: Fun place. Would be even better if it didn't reek of cigarettes inside :)

name: Arhavi Resort Hotel
address: Musazde mahallesi Atatürk bulvarı, caddesi, 08200 Arhavi/Artvin
text: Rooms are dirty, food is mediocre, staff is rude.

name: Pilunç Çay Evi
text: I can honestly say it was the best sucuk sandwich I've ever had. Everything we ate was absolutely delicious. The mıhlama was also great. We ate until we were in a food coma.

name: Pokut Doğa Konukevi
text: Their desserts are really good. We especially loved the blueberry one.

name: Hevas cay
text: A more affordable spot compared to the venues in Ayder. Ambiance is standard. Makes sense for having tea or coffee.

name: Ahşap Loya Otel
address: ÇAMLIHEMŞİN YOLU 14. KM, KÖPRÜ AYAĞI MEVKİ NO:91, 53780 Çamlıhemşin/Rize
text:
+ The rooms have a nice ambiance, a hotel in harmony with nature.
+ Dinner was very delicious. Great homestyle regional dishes.
+ The evening entertainment was good. The tulum and horon were fun.
- They told us the tap water was drinkable. Since our bottled water ran out, we had to drink it at night and unfortunately got food poisoning afterward. Do not drink the water.
- Unfortunately the room cleaning wasn't done well. There was a lot of leftover trash from the previous guest.

name: Amourette Urla
text: Leyt closed down and has been completely replaced by Amourette. Unfortunately the food quality has dropped significantly. The service staff works fast but the kitchen is really slow, and you have to wait at least an hour for your food. The lovely Leyt's menu has also become very generic. Disappointing.

name: SARPI | Georgian-Turkish border
text: The worst border crossing I've ever been through in my life. Narrow, disorganized, chaotic, people climbing over each other. Packed like sardines. Do yourself a favor and fly in, skip this place entirely.

name: Palovit Waterfall
text: An overhyped waterfall. Not worth the drive. The other historical and natural attractions in the area are genuinely beautiful, but this one is unnecessarily overhyped.

name: Borçka Karagöl Nature Park
text: An overhyped location. The natural beauty is nice, but there are much prettier lakes in our country. If you live in the area, it's fine for occasional visits, but there's no point including it in Black Sea tours and bringing people here.

name: Santana Resort Otel
text: It's in a very peaceful and enjoyable spot location-wise, right by the sea. The first room we got was very stuffy, so we switched right away and the second one was fine.

name: Nova Suite Hotel
text: Right next to the airport, rooms aren't bad, could've been a bit cleaner.

name: AKPINAR YILDIZLI RESTAURANT
address: Yıldızlı Toki Konutları Yanı, Yıldızlı, Sahil Yolu Cad, 61300 Akçaabat/Trabzon
text: The view is genuinely beautiful, but that's all there is. The food was pretty mediocre, and many of the appetizers had gone sour. The Akpınar special meat dish was also bland and sour. We were not happy.

name: Artı Nirvana Cafe Restaurant
text: The regional dishes were quite authentic. Some weren't to our taste (taflan pickle), but overall we enjoyed the herb-based dishes (sakarca muhlama, yayla wrap). The owners were very attentive and explained the details of each dish and even offered us complimentary bites.

name: Terme Ulusoy Tesisleri
text: Food is standard, the amazon dessert is nothing special.

name: Park Inn by Radisson Samsun
text: Rooms are nice and spacious, hotel is clean, breakfast was mediocre.

name: Lokal
text:
The sea bass and seafood şevketi bostan was great. The appetizers were average overall, a few of them were stale. It was a bummer that there was only one white wine option.

A decent restaurant if you want to eat fish.

name: Murano's Kitchen Alsancak
address: The Alsancak Apt, Kültür, Ümran Baradan Sokak No:7 A, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text:
- They allow smoking indoors.
- The dishes are missing a sauce element. The pizza was a bit dry, and the lasagna was too doughy. There's a lack of quality in the ingredients.
- The wine menu is nice and extensive.

name: Muà Alsancak - İzmir (Muà Gelateria d’italia)
address: Kültür Mahallesi, 1383 sokak Tülin Apt. NO:7/B Konak ilçesi, 35000 Konak/İzmir
text:
The ice cream quality is really great, you can tell they've put real effort into it. The pistachio one in particular was excellent. They haven't cut corners on quality.

Don't pay attention to bad reviews saying it's too expensive or they charge extra. The pricing menu is written in detail behind the register. There's no sneaky charging going on, they've listed everything. I don't see any problem with them charging extra for chocolate sauce since they use quality chocolate. We're just used to having chocolate sauce with 2% actual chocolate poured on our ice cream for free, so when we see quality we don't get it.

name: TERUAR URLA
text:
A restaurant that doesn't give you an a la carte option if you go with more than 5 people, only the tasting menu. I respect that decision, but it was a bit disappointing that they didn't mention it during the reservation.

Food quality is generally good. There are different plates. Some were skippable while others were really great. The okra vellutata was truly amazing, a signature dish. We also really liked the Cretan zucchini. The desserts at the end were standard though.

The tasting menu is filling overall. But there's way too much downtime between courses. We sat down at 8 and didn't finish all the plates until 11:30.

The wine menu is quite extensive, and the sommelier was really attentive. The staff is very friendly and fun. You can tell they're happy with their working conditions.

The bathroom doors open inward. That doesn't meet safety standards for an emergency.

Whether this place is worth the money at today's prices is debatable, but I guess that could be said about most places given the current economic situation.

name: Bozdoğan Pide Salonu Nazilli
address: NAZİLLİ EVRANLI MEZARLIĞI ÇAPRAZI, Zafer, Ordu Cd. NO:164/1A D:1B, 09010 Nazilli/Aydın
text: The overall pide flavor is really great, we loved the döner pide. The tahini dessert pide is something you just have to experience, words don't do it justice.

name: Arpège Patisserie
text: Their desserts are truly outstanding. They're so good you'll eat yourself into a sugar coma :) The coffees are also great. A charming and enjoyable place.

name: Kuzu Döner
address: Hacı İsa, Mustafa Kemal Paşa Cd. No:10, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: The döner tastes good, food comes out fast. A solid pick for a quick meal.

name: Statera Şarapçılık | Statera Winery
text: The newest winery in the area. Some of their wines are good, some still have a way to go, but they'll get much better down the road. They've got potential. The staff is very attentive, and the tasting setting is nice. Definitely a must-visit.

name: Carre Gusto Pizza
address: Alsancak, Mahmut Esat Bozkurt Cd. no:43/A, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text: Incredibly delicious, reasonably priced for the quality and ingredients, and doesn't sit heavy thanks to the long fermentation. We loved it. Our favorite was the chicken pesto one.

name: Steak Concept
address: Hacı İsa, Necati Cumalı Cd. no: 17, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: The burger flavors aren't bad, but it's not exactly a standout concept either. Some of the fries came stale. It was also disappointing that they don't have beer as a drink option.

name: Küçük Yayla Kızların Yeri
text: Nice place, the gözleme portions are quite big. We liked the cheesy one the most. A pleasant stop for a break on the road.

name: Ali Bar Restorant
text: You definitely need to place your order a day in advance. They go to Marmaris to do the shopping. It's a pretty rustic spot. The food is excellent. The seafood in particular was incredibly enjoyable. One of the most original places I've ever been to. If we're ever in the area again, we'll definitely come back.

name: Yucel fish restaurant
text:
The appetizers weren't bad but the seafood and fish were really great. The sea bass in particular was super fresh and delicious.

The mushroom and avcı böreği were also tasty.

name: Captain İbrahim's
text: The best Çökertme Kebab I've ever had in my life. Even though the portions are huge, the flavor was so good I ate two portions. We'll have to come back :) definitely don't pass through here without eating.

name: Benjamin Otel
text:
The rooms are nicely designed, the fridge is pretty big. The only thing is the corners near the bed are a bit of a booby trap when you walk by :)

Breakfast is good, filling. The rooms, especially the ground floor ones, don't heat up well. The owners are warm people.

We had a hot water problem, there's an issue with the water pressure.

name: LİMON GÜMÜŞLÜK RESTAURANT
text: The setting and atmosphere are really beautiful, but we found the food average. The appetizers weren't bad. The wine selection is nice and the prices are reasonable. Worth visiting for the sunset vibes and good wine.

name: Bake My Day Gümüşlük
text: The location is pretty terrible, impossible to sit and eat in the heat. Some of their desserts were good while others we found average.

name: Nazar Gurme Ortakent - Lahmacun - Pide - Kebap - Izgara - Çorba
address: Ortakent Çarşısı, Müskebi, Kemer Sk. No:8, 48420 Bodrum/Muğla
text: Their lahmacuns are still very good, portions are big. The walnut one is our favorite.

name: Bitez Dondurma
text: Ice cream quality has sadly gone downhill. Nothing left of its former glory.

name: Yula Bodrum - Drink & Chill
text: Great place, spacious, breezy, right by the sea. The cocktails are generally decent but a bit too sweet. We liked the On Numara and Black Mustache.

name: La Pasion Bodrum
address: Çarşı Mahallesi Atatürk cd. Uslu sok. No:8, 48400 Bodrum/Muğla
text: Hot tapas overall were really good. We liked the burrata, crispy cheek borek, and mushroom. Sangria was solid, margarita was a bit heavy. Despite a couple of mezes we didn't love, we were happy with the food overall.

name: Barba Urla
address: Kalabak, Mareşal Fevzi Çakmak Blv. No:328 B Kıyıda, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text:
The place is really beautiful. The view, the vibe, the atmosphere, all amazing.

The food is generally good, just the portions are a bit small. On a day where we went heavy on seafood, we liked the hummus and octopus the most.

The service could be a bit more professional.

name: TCDD camp
text:
As of 2024, the price of 400 is pretty steep given the conditions. They've hiked it up significantly in 2 years. Other than that, the setting is nice, the sea is genuinely clean and enjoyable, and even when it's windy the waves don't hit this spot too hard.

The showers and changing cabins are clean. The toilets could be cleaner.

The no-alcohol policy is ridiculous. It would be so much better if they allowed it.

Overall, given the lack of beach spots in the Iskele-Cesmealtı area, it fills a gap.

name: Ephesus Ancient Greek Theatre
text: Think twice before going to a concert here. The setting is truly beautiful but the sound system is terrible, there are insane parking and ticket lines at entry and exit, and the stones have been baking in the sun all day so they burn like crazy. And most importantly, there is definitely a scorpion problem. After someone 5-6 rows ahead of us screamed about a scorpion, we shined a light under our seats and spotted scorpions right away. When we told security on the way out, they casually said yeah there are 3-4 scorpions at every concert. That level of nonchalance and that level of bad organization is unacceptable.

name: 7 Bilgeler Winery & Wineyards
text:
You can do a paid tasting until 5 PM. The person conducting the tasting was really knowledgeable and attentive. Their wines are generally tasty, but the best one is hands down the Pythagoras. We'll definitely go try the restaurant one day too.

2 years later we got the chance to try the restaurant as well. The food is generally good. Seafood papardelle, Cretan zucchini, and mücver quiche were our favorites.

name: Hotel Gölmar Beach
text: The rooms are dated, the hotel is very crowded, and overall the food is insufficient. There are even times when they run out. It's a buffet concept but the food is really mediocre. The sea and beach are nice, but unfortunately that's about it.

name: Marmaris Bal Evi
text: A nice museum for seeing how pine honey is made. You can see sample beehives and how they work. You can also buy lab-tested clean honey, royal jelly, propolis and more.

name: Cafe Savas
text: One of the rare places where prices aren't a ripoff. Everywhere we asked had no tea service, but this place did. Overall the atmosphere is nice, staff is attentive.

name: Bayır Çınarı Tabiat Anıtı
text: An amazing place. Absolutely a must-visit. The atmosphere around the 2,000-year-old plane tree is incredible.

name: Rafet Baba Restaurant
text:
The sea is really beautiful. The food was good, we liked the fish and octopus, the Aegean appetizer was nice too.

If you're planning to eat and then move to the sunbed area, dealing with the staff over there can be a bit tricky.

name: Manej Urla | Otel | Binicilik
text:
Our 6-year-old nephew took a 10-minute mini horseback riding tour and both the instructor and the staff were really attentive. We were genuinely happy with it. Price-wise it's a bit more expensive than other farms in the area, but it's a nice setting for a good time.

We also got to try the restaurant much later. While we found the hot appetizers average, the pizzas were quite good. The veggie pizza especially was really tasty flavor-wise, and the portions are big. Just don't eat too much, it sits heavy :)

name: İstifçi Urla
text:
Great atmosphere, the food is also good. The pesto pasta and meat-based sharing appetizers were solid. The beef short rib hot appetizer in particular was excellent. The appetizer we ordered expecting potatoes turned out to be all processed meat, but overall what we ate was good. Still, it's worth asking exactly what's in things when ordering items with different ingredients, to avoid surprises.

The by-the-glass wine selections are nice too. A good option for dinner.

name: Jale Urla
text:
The food, service, and atmosphere are quite nice. Prices are reasonable compared to the market. The by-the-glass wine selections in particular are good and well-priced.

Some dishes were great while others were just okay. You should ask about the contents before ordering. What we ordered as sushi turned out to be an unwrapped bowl due to our own ignorance :) still tasted good though. Our favorite hot appetizer was the arancini balls. The pesto pasta was good too, but the seafood risotto came out different from what we expected.

name: 1890 Urla
text:
The atmosphere is nice, the service is genuinely great. But overall we weren't happy with what we ate. The burger was standard, the ribs were too dried out. The sharing appetizers we ordered were better. You could skip the mains and go with a mix of shared hot appetizers instead.

The by-the-glass wine selections are mediocre, and the cocktails were standard too.

name: Aren Beach & Club
text:
The sea is nice but you should go on calm days. On windy days it makes way more sense to swim from the Iskele side.

The minimum spend isn't too high but the menu is pretty expensive. The cheapest beer was 300 TL as of June 2024.

The food is unfortunately mediocre.

If they gave the place a proper overhaul and improved the menu and food, it could actually be a really great spot. It's the only beach club in Urla.

name: Basta La Pasta
text: Flavor isn't bad for the price, but the ingredient quality could be a bit better.

name: Girit Pastanesi 1950
text: Scraping the kazandibi tray clean is an incredible flavor experience.

name: Mezze House İki Kule
address: Hoşnudiye, İsmet İnönü-1 Blv No:153 D:J, 26130 Tepebaşı/Eskişehir
text: The seafood hot appetizers were generally way too greasy and didn't suit my palate. The cold appetizers weren't bad though.

name: Varuna Gezgin Cafe - Eskişehir - Kantin
text: Used to be a place we really loved. At their new location the ventilation system is terrible. Even though we sat in the non-smoking area, our clothes reeked of cigarettes and our throats were burning.

name: Yum Yum Noodle House
text: Not bad for the price but the sushi flavors in general didn't really suit my palate, and I found the fried ice cream average too.

name: Bal Kahvaltı
text: Their spread breakfast is great, more than filling, and the product quality is solid overall.

name: Karacabey Bursa Kebap Evi - Oksijen 0135
address: OKSİJEN 0135, İSTANBUL YÖNÜ, Seyran, Seyran Sk No:599/1, 16700 Karacabey/Bursa
text:
This place is the answer to why you should check ratings before eating somewhere. We were hungry on the road so we walked in without looking and the food couldn't have been worse. We ordered hummus and it came sour. When we said there might be a problem and asked them to check, they said it sat out too long in the heat today so it went bad. I've never seen that level of casualness anywhere :) The soup, iskender, etc. had no flavor. The iskender that came as a "1.5 portion" had less than 100 grams of meat. Hair came out of the iskender.

If you want to waste your money and have a miserable experience, I recommend stopping by.

name: Özcanlar Köfte | Beyazköy Şube
text: Portions are weak, prices are high relative to the portions. Their yogurt was good as always, but the other dishes were standard.

name: Papadopoulos Eleftherios "Halvas Eleftheriou"
address: EO Dramas Kavalas 14 χιλ, Ag. Athanasios 661 00, Greece
text: Perfect pastry! Mostly healthy food. Very friendly owners.

name: Mehana Bai Kotse
address: кв. Стария град, ul. "Velyan Ognev" 1, 2770 Bansko, Bulgaria
text: Really good food! We really enjoyed their soups.

name: Central bar&dinner
text: Good coffee!

name: Pavazh Restaurant
text: Fried zucchini was awesome.

name: Woodchurch Wine
address: Susan's Hill, Woodchurch, Ashford TN26 3RE, United Kingdom
text: This place is just great. They have really good wines + atmosphere is just great. Really good view. Owners are also paying attention to all customers. Great experience, definitely worth to visit!

name: Wayfarer Wines
address: Wayfarer Wines Highfield Farm Lower, Susan's Hill, Woodchurch, Ashford TN26 3TA, United Kingdom
text: A little family-owned vineyard. If you are around, good to do wine tasting. Our favorite was the wine with Bacchus grape.

name: Balfour Winery
address: Balfour Winery, Five Oak Ln, Staplehurst, Tonbridge TN12 0HT, United Kingdom
text: Great atmosphere, good designed vineyards. They have really good bubbles, definitely worth to visit!

name: The Bull at Benenden
address: 5 Orchard Ct, The St, Benenden, Cranbrook TN17 4DE, United Kingdom
text: A traditional pub/restaurant. We ate hamburgers and chips and they were quite delicious.

name: Bedales of Borough Market
address: 5 Bedale St, London SE1 9AL, United Kingdom
text:
Great atmosphere, appetizers and selection of glass of wines.

Arancini and octopus was great!

name: Baccalà Seafood & Wine - Restaurant London Bridge
address: Unit B3, 194 Bermondsey St, London SE1 3TQ, United Kingdom
text:
Delicious fried squid and seafood pasta. All the food we eat was just great. They also have a good selection of wines.

One problem was the inside of the restaurant was a little bit unventilated. Food smell will be all on your clothes.

name: Trivet
text:
They have really good options in terms of wine menu. You can drink any wine all around the world. You can just go to drink a glass of wine.

About food, they have some good options, but some of the appetizers were just ok. Expectations from a two star chef restaurant is more from that.

name: Farmer J London Bridge
address: Unit SU49, Bermondsey St, London SE1 2TF, United Kingdom
text: Healthy and super delicious bowls. They have quite options, which all of them were nice. Cookies are also best :)

name: Residence Inn by Marriott London Bridge
address: 201, 211 Long Ln, London SE1 4PN, United Kingdom
text:
Rooms were enough big, kitchens are also nice for long stays. But rooms were a little bit dirty when we got it. There were leftover towels from previous guests.

Breakfast was quite nice. Location is great, just 5-10 minutes to the London Bridge.

name: The Dickens Inn
address: 50 St Katharine's Way, London E1W 1LA, United Kingdom
text: It has a good atmosphere and building. Food is just bar food, it was ok but not too complex.

name: Vagabond Wines Monument
address: 51 Gracechurch St, London EC3V 0EH, United Kingdom
text: Just a great place to taste different wines. Good food, really good atmosphere, great selection of wines!

name: Vagabond Wines Charlotte Street, Fitzrovia
address: 25 Charlotte St., London W1T 1RW, United Kingdom
text: Comparing to the other Vagabond's, this one is a little bit small. It was airless when visited on a cold winter evening. Maybe it could be good in the spring/summer, but you can look into others in cold weather.

name: Cyberdog
address: 45 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London NW1 8AH, United Kingdom
text: Just an amazing place! :) If you are into cyberpunk, definitely worth to visit, they have amazing stuff.

name: Turf Tavern
address: 4 Bath Pl, Oxford OX1 3SU, United Kingdom
text: The Turf Tavern food looked really appealing but came out mediocre. The seafood risotto was basically just cooked rice :)) The squid was way too oily, really burned my stomach.

name: Christ Church
address: St Aldate's, Oxford OX1 1DP, United Kingdom
text: Good to see, but the price is high comparing to what to have inside.

name: The Alchemist Oxford
address: Westgate, Bonn Square, Oxford OX1 1TR, United Kingdom
text: Nice atmosphere, good service, really nice desserts.

name: Pamukkale Wines Selimiye
address: Selimiye Mah. Buruncuk Sok. No:86, D:1, 48700 Marmaris/Muğla
text: At a wine bar, honestly, we expect them to know more about wine than the average bar :) unfortunately there wasn't much difference on that front. Other than that, the prices are genuinely reasonable and it's great to have so many options. We already love Pamukkale wines. A nice atmosphere for hanging out in the evening.

name: Tamara Beach
address: Selimiye, Hanım Pinari Mevki No:11, 48700 Marmaris/Muğla
text: A place you'll either love or hate. We loved it and hung out here the entire 3 days of our vacation. The food at Amerikalı's is amazing :) don't leave Selimiye without trying the seafood casserole, I'd say.

name: Selimiye Sardunya Restaurant & Otel
address: Selimiye, Hanım Pınarı Sok No 9, 48710 Marmaris/Muğla
text: Unfortunately there's no trace of what it used to be. The service is terrible, the food has become standard. Even though it was a weekday, they couldn't bring the food on time or even get the orders right. The setting and ambiance are absolutely gorgeous, but when it comes to a restaurant's most essential qualities, service and flavor, they're unfortunately in really bad shape compared to how they used to be.

name: Hanımpınarı Butik Otel
address: Selimiye, Hanım pınar küme evler no:97/A, 48700 Marmaris/Muğla
text: A wonderful family-run place, we were happy with it in every way. We're planning to go again this year.

name: Asarcık Bağları
text:
They have a great story behind them. The setting, the view, wonderful.

Prices are a bit steep for what you get, partly the effect of being in a touristy area and selling mostly to foreigners. Their wines have a distinctive character. We especially liked the whites. The Marsanne-Roussanne is a must-try.

name: Mersinli Ciğerci Musa
text: Food quality was good, even though it was Ramadan and crowded the guys brought everything fast. The appetizers weren't great, and the liver had a slight smell but the taste itself was fine. Parking is a serious problem but thankfully they have valet.

name: Petek Pastahanesi İzmir Bayraklı
address: Avcılar Exclusive, Mansuroğlu Mahallesi Adliye Meyd, 288/1 Sk. No:9 D:1 Blok, 35535 Bayraklı/İzmir
text: The food and atmosphere are really nice, desserts were good at first but got a bit too heavy after a while. Portions are big so be careful. A traditional dessert shop.

name: Balıkçım Urla
address: Hacı İsa, Erdoğan Ker Cd. No: 9 /1, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: The whiting skewers were melt-in-your-mouth good. We went twice and one time the service was great, the other time we had quite a few issues. Overall the flavors are good, if you catch them on a good day you'll have a nice seafood experience.

name: EVDE BURGER - PUB
text: The atmosphere is really nice, burgers are fresh and tasty. Their style is a bit different, it might not be what you're used to so it may not fully satisfy you, but they absolutely nail what they're going for quality-wise.

name: Nava Kitchen
text: As a pasta lover, we weren't very happy with what we had. Even though we got the pasta in the Parmesan wheel, the cheese level was low. We didn't love the sauce either. The side bar was closed when we went so there was no alcohol available. It's a shame that a place with this concept doesn't have a liquor license.

name: da’s cantina
text: All the food is super tasty. For drinks, their spicy passion fruit margaritas are incredible. We're obsessed with their nachos.

name: Turşurla Meze
address: Hacı İsa, Bülent Baratalı Blv. 45/1A, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: We got our appetizers and turkey from here for New Year's. Every appetizer we had was excellent. Their plum pickles are amazing too. All the food is fresh. You can buy with complete peace of mind.
While the service is a little bit slow, the wine options from the fridge is just amazing!

name: RODIZINHO Bar & Grill Puerto Madero
address: Juana Manuela Gorriti 849, C1107 Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
text: All you can eat salad bar and unlimited meat is 22 usd. We both enjoyed the bar and the meats served. They also have good wines to taste.

name: Almacén Porteño
address: Magallanes 855, C1166 Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
text: You can taste wines inside

name: Santiago Wine Club
text: A really good wine store. They recommended the best wine that we drank at our travel. Really worth visit if you want to try some local wines.

name: Bocanáriz
address: José Victorino Lastarria 276, 8320126 Santiago, Región Metropolitana, Chile
text: Awesome food, really good selection of wines. Perfect place to dine in!

name: Hotel Panamericano
text: Really good location and breakfast, but rooms are really old and it was smelling of cigarette.

name: Viña Cousiño Macul
address: Av. Quilin 7100, 7921077 Peñalolén, Región Metropolitana, Chile
text: Good wines and really nice tasting tour :) really crowded especially in the afternoon in the weekends

name: Viña Concha y Toro
address: Av. Virginia Subercaseaux 210, Pirque, Región Metropolitana, Chile
text:
Nice tour! Definitely worth to visit.
We selected MCC tours but the 2nd part of the tasting was not too promising. So if you are into wine, you can look into different tastings rather than MCC.

name: Coca Museum
text: Museum is Spanish only, but still they have a really good cafe to try. They have lots of different varieties mixed with coca, such as beer, wine, and shots. Definitely worth to try!

name: Metropolis Antik Kenti
text:
A neglected ancient city, left to its own fate. The theater and mosaics are worth seeing but definitely don't attempt to climb the acropolis, because there's absolutely nothing at the top.

If you're passing through the area, you can stop by for a quick look, but there's no need to plan a trip just for this. They really need to give the place a proper cleanup and restoration.

name: Lucien Arkas Bağları
address: Yazıbaşı sokak, Kuşçuburun, Mimarbaşı Sinan Cd. No:88, 35860 Torbalı/İzmir
text:
Nice atmosphere, quality wines. We especially loved the 2020 vintage red made from Marselan and the 2021 vintage white made from the Sauvignon Blanc-Trebbiano blend.

Apparently they don't do tastings on weekends. You can only dine at the restaurant and order by the glass or bottle.

name: Zeynel Ergin Gevrek Fırını
text:
I can't comment since I don't know what it used to be like, but in its current state we found it a bit overhyped. It's very crowded but your turn comes fast, so we didn't have much of a problem there. If the weather's nice there seems to be plenty of outdoor seating, with high turnover.

The simit is a classic simit, nothing extra special about it. For boyoz I'd prefer Dostlar Fırını. The kumru was good. The tahini dessert simits were really great, probably the best thing there. But we didn't find the extreme difference or flavor that would justify it being at the top of every breakfast list for Izmir.

name: Hay Pan
text:
