If you are into wines and want to discover Bolivian wines, this is the place. They have different choices of both glasses and bottles in terms of variety. Owners do not know English but you can talk via translate apps.

Food was not too promising. If possible, only go for wine :)

name: Mitru Express
address: Zona Miraflores, Av. Saavedra Calle, casimiro corrales N° 1013, La Paz, Bolivia
text: Really good hotel. New and large rooms, shower is awesomely designed. Both breakfast and dinners are really great. We really enjoyed ribs, ravioli and spaghetti. Selection of wines are also good.

name: Sky and Salt Hotel
address: C. Josefina Mayorga y, Uyuni, Bolivia
text: Good hotel, nice rooms, food was ok.

name: Taypi Uta Тiwanaku
text: Good open buffet!

name: Lokal Alsancak
text: For a fine dining concept, all the dishes fall a notch short on flavor. I can't say the food was bad, but none of it gave us a wow moment either. Just because they're short-staffed, the food comes out really slowly. If you arrive hungry you'll stay hungry for quite a while. Our most enjoyable dish was the lamb loin, while the New York steak was genuinely flavorless.

name: Pier Balık Pişiricisi
text: Tasty food, fair prices, and you can order takeout too. The seafood şevketi bostan was really great.

name: Hotel Qalasaya
text: Old rooms, little elevator which is not enough for this kind of capacity for sure. Meals were ok.

name: Uros Floating Islands
text: Definitely worth visiting. Different culture, different lifestyle. The experience will broaden your horizon.

name: HOSTAL SAN PEDRO - SAN PABLO (amplia cochera, wifi, tv cable) apto para motorhome
address: Av. Panamericana s/n, San Pablo 08250, Peru
text: Good coffee! They also have llamas and alpacas which you can play with :)

name: Mercado Central de San Pedro
text: Cheaper than comparing to the souvenirs they have in Lima. But they change USD within lower rate comparing to the other places. So going with SOL makes much more sense.

name: El Mesón de Don Tomas
text: Nice food (alpaca was good), good pisco (we loved mango-peach one), and good live music!

name: Hotel Monasterio San Pedro
text: Good breakfast, mystic place, good location.

name: MachuQolqa Viewpoint
text: Perfect viewpoint. Also the souvenirs are really cheap here.

name: Historic Sanctuary of Machu Picchu
text: The best world wonder I have ever seen.

name: Restaurant Fortaleza
address: Imperio de los Incas 601, Aguas Calientes 08681, Peru
text: Terrible dessert (cheesecake) and coffee

name: Toto's House
address: Imperio de los Incas, Aguas Calientes 08681, Peru
text: Good open buffet, nice live music!

name: Monte Amazonico Lodge
address: Rió Madre de Dios km - 20, Puerto Maldonado 17001, Peru
text: Really good environment. Rooms are ok. Food is nice, service is great. Sometimes they use refined water when gallons are finished.

name: Karamba Resto-Bar
text: Seafood quinoa paella and octopus grill was awesome! But you can stay away from squid :)

name: Hotel Villa Jazmin
text: Good food at restaurant, rooms are nice too but they do not have any sound isolation, you can hear everyone from restaurant at your rooms.
Nice rooms and location however service is low quality. They have 4 star but they cannot serve wine in the restaurant because they do not have a corkscrew :)

name: Curador Wine Bar & Shop
text: Nice food! We really enjoyed mushroom pizzas. They have big portions. Service is super professional. Served wines were mehh, it could be improved.

name: La Esquina Bar de Vinos
text: They do not have any glass options, you can only buy as bottle. I mean, what kind of wine bar is that?

name: Perfect Blend Cata y Maridaje
text:
They do have glass options. They also have wine tasting but it is expensive comparing to the having same wines as glass; so choose glass options instead of tasting.

They do not know English, however you can understand each other within Google Translate.

name: CHOCOMUSEO
text: Perfect place for pisco based drink tasting! We loved passion fruit and goldenberry ones.

name: MUSEO DEL PISCO - LIMA
text: Nice place but they do not know English unfortunately.

name: El Mesón del Inca
address: Club La Unión, Portal de Escribanos, Pje. Sta. Rosa 117, Lima 15001, Peru
text: Nice location and view. Food was good, especially snackers. They do not have Peruvian wine as glass but once we asked, they served, so thank you for that! :)

name: Best Western Plus 93 Park Hotel
text: Location was a little bit far from the historical center, but all other things were perfect. Really enjoyed the rooms as well as the breakfast.

name: il Sud Moda
address: Caferağa, Sarraf Ali Sk. No 15, 34710 Kadıköy/İstanbul
text: The risotto and pasta were really good. Hot appetizers and wine selection were also excellent. An enjoyable restaurant.

name: Develi 1912
address: Fenerbahçe, Münir Nurettin Selçuk Cd. Kalamış Yat Limanı, 34726 Kadıköy/İstanbul
text: Great kebabs, relaxed atmosphere, fast service. The pistachio kebab was superb.

name: DEEPNATURE TRAVEL
address: METROPOL İSTANBUL (ATAŞEHİR) A Blok No:2E, K:21 D:331, 34758 Ataşehir/İstanbul
text: One of the best agencies I've ever worked with. Thanks to Arzum hanim, she's genuinely very attentive throughout the tour process once you sign up, and she handles your special requests at the highest level. Whenever I'm going to book a new tour, I always consult her and she finds great options. We've joined 3 tours through them so far and were very happy with all of them.

name: 1853 The Wine Experience
address: Bolívar 1015, C1066 Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
text: One of the best wine bars I have ever visited so far! Really good selection of wines as glass and good bottles to be bought. The owners were amazing and quite helpful to tell about Argentinian wines. If we ever visit Buenos Aires again, we will definitely stop by again! :)

name: Tombul Tavuk
address: Kardeşler Apartmanı, Feyzullah, Bağdat Cad. No:236A, 34854 Maltepe/İstanbul
text: Tasty food, wings are great. The Mexican sauce chicken was a bit too spicy though :)

name: Chrismar Hotel Livingstone
text: The atmosphere of the hotel is great. Like a heaven in the morning. But cannot say same for evening. Mosquitoes are everywhere in the room and the bednet is not useful at all. Food is not too delicious too.

name: Zambezi Boat Club
text: Amazing view for the sunset! Good and tourist friendly environment. Liked the Zambian beers. You need local currency money though.

name: Debonairs Pizza Livingstone
text: Place is a little bit dirty, pizzas are ok to eat but you can look for alternatives

name: The Lookout Cafe - Wild Horizons
text:
Really great view! I mean, you can hangout there for a day.

Service is slow but it is usual at Africa, so you can get used to it. Good coffee.

name: Marula Cafe
address: Shop 1 Centre, corner of Livingstone Way and Parkway Drive, Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe, Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe
text: Good food. We really like ostrich fillets and crocodile crochet. Only we suffered is we asked wine which looks like Zambian on the menu to the employees. They respond as the wine is Zambian but it is actually made from a Zambian winemaker working and living in South Africa. So I do not enjoy that kind of misleading

name: Cresta Sprayview
text: Good location, rooms are old and entrances are really small, employees are great. Breakfast is nice, open buffet for evening is meh

name: Kalahari Tours
text: We had a one day tour including safari in forest and river which was great and I highly recommend it!

name: The Coffee Buzz
text: Good food as open buffet! Hake fish and salads were really great.

name: Mugg & Bean OR Tambo International Airport Landside
address: Shop IDR19, O.R. Tambo International Airport, O.R. Tambo Airport Road, Landside, O.R. Tambo, Kempton Park, 1627, South Africa
text: Desserts are super big, it could be shared by 2 or 3. Good coffee, delicious desserts

name: MAMASAMBA
address: Cradock Ave, Rosebank, Johannesburg, 2198, South Africa
text: Awesome food and really quick service. We really enjoyed salmon appetizers and sushis!

name: Acid Food & Wine Bar
address: 19 4th Ave, Parktown North, Randburg, 2193, South Africa
text: Awesome food and perfect selection of wines! Glass noodle spring rolls were great. Salmon was really good too.

name: Gerakaris Family Wines
address: REEA Complex, Gate B, Marlborough Ave, Craighall Park, Randburg, 2196, South Africa
text: Place and design is awesome. Really cool place for relaxing. However wines are not too good, you cannot compare within other similar priced SA wines.

name: Ocean Basket Sandton City
address: Sandton City, Food Court, L4, Sandton Dr, Sandhurst, Sandton, 2196, South Africa
text: Perfect as always!

name: Sip Liquor Sandton
address: Atrium on 5th Parking Entrance, Shopping Centre, 5th St, Sandhurst, Sandton, 2146, South Africa
text: Good shop! A lot of options to buy good wines.

name: Holiday Inn Express Sandton - Woodmead by IHG
address: Woodmead North Office Park, 54 Maxwell Dr, Sandton, 2191, South Africa
text: Location is a little bit far to center. Rooms are big but it could be refurbished. Service was ok.

name: Lesedi Cultural Village
text: The village is mostly touristical. I was expecting more local experience but it is mostly designed for tourists.

name: The Hussar Grill Stellenbosch
address: Shop G3, Good Hope Building, 23 Plein St, Stellenbosch Central, Stellenbosch, 7600, South Africa
text: Good steak and appetizers! Their own branded bottled wine was ok but not too perfect. Service is a little bit slow.

name: Stellenbosch Wine Bar
address: Church St, Stellenbosch Central, Stellenbosch, 7599, South Africa
text: Helpful employees. Good wine list. Only disadvantage was food smell inside of the bar which is bad for the tasting.

name: Fairview Wine and Cheese
address: Suid-Agter-Paarl Rd, Suider, Paarl, 7646, South Africa
text:
One of the best tasting I have ever had in my life. Really good wines, helpful narrator and perfect cheeses!

2009 shiraz was our favorite. It is just perfect.

name: The Bailey
address: 91 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town, 8000, South Africa
text:
They have a really good wine list. Bar is really nice.
The employees were really professional, friendly and helpful.

name: Openwine
address: 72 Wale St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa
text:
Really nice atmosphere. It was super crowded. Live music is nice. They have tasting for 4 wines to 250 rand.

They were out of pinotage and cape blend when we visited, which was not applicable for a south african wine bar :)

name: Seaforth Restaurant
address: Beach, Seaforth Rd, Simon's Town, 7975, South Africa
text: Soup was great. Service a little bit slow. But general food quality is nice.

name: The Capetonian
address: Pier Place, Heerengracht St, Foreshore, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa
text:
Water leakage on nearly all of the rooms in the bathroom.
Also from raining, water came from windows.
There were no converters left in the hotel.
Old rooms.

name: Ocean Basket V&A Waterfront
address: Shop 222, V&A Waterfront, Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa
text: Portions are really huge. We got mixed seafood plate and that was awesome! Also their selection of sparkling wine is also good too.

name: Ferrymans Irish Tavern
address: Locomotive Shed, Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa
text: Nice wine menu! They have 20-30 wine options as a glass. Really impressed that performance from an Irish Tavern.

name: Pemba Mozambican Restaurant
address: Old, Shop 10 Chameleon Village R104, Rustenburg rd, Damdoryn, Hartbeespoort, 0216, South Africa
text: Atmosphere is really nice, colorful design. We stopped by for coffee. Service was super slow. Coffee was not good quality too.

name: The Kingdom Resort
address: R556, South Village, Pilanesberg National Park, 0316, South Africa
text:
Good and fancy rooms, place is nice too. Restaurant is nice too, chicken pizza was great. Bar is closing early.

At the rooms, long wait time for hot water.

name: Secondo
text: Big portions, tasty food. Pizzas and salad are really good. Lasagna is nice too but not quite like real lasagna. Tiramisu isn't bad flavor-wise but wasn't quite like real tiramisu either :) still, definitely a restaurant worth trying.

name: Seven 2 Seven
text: Ice cream is too sweet and creamy. Cannot compare with natural ones.

name: Καφέ Αίθριο στο Γεντί Κουλέ. Cafe Aithrio at Yedikule.
text: Really nice atmosphere. Orders are processed a little bit late but smoothies were really delicious

name: Hard Rock Cafe
text: Comparing the prices of other similar restaurants in porto, this is way more expensive. Shrimps are perfect as always.

name: Yialo Yialo Restaurant Mykonos
text: Good sea. Nice place to hangout.

name: Mykonos Vioma Organic Farm
address: Maou Doubakia, PO 844, Ano Mera 846 00, Greece
text: A good place to have a tasting. Wines are ok but the place is really nice. Worth to try

name: Sarakiniko
text: Perfect place to see. Just a unique place! Definitely worth it.

name: New Malion Restaurant
text: Good for view and coffee

name: Boutari Winery Santorini
text: Best in the area! Awesome wines. Really loved all of them. Especially 1879 Legacy one was perfect.

name: Venetsanos Winery
text: Good view! Our favorite was rose one.

name: Santo Wines
text: Really good view! Premium tasting menu is good to go.

name: Kleanthi Olive Farm
text: Good for the facility tour however they only have 1 type of olive oil. Go hungry because they have lots of things to eat with :)

name: Domaine Paterianakis- Κτήμα Πατεριανάκη
text: Good whites. Really love the muscat one! Good tasting and presentation.

name: Lyrarakis Winery & Tasting Room
address: Alagni, Arkalochori, 70300, Alagni, Heraklion, Crete 703 00, Greece
text: Really good wines. Especially loved Mandilari one. Dafni is also really original, worth to try.

name: Хотел-ресторант Градината
text: Delicious food, entertaining live music :) only have 1 wine option which is kinda house wine for their own

name: Restaurant Waterside houses
text: Atmosphere is great. Service is a little bit slow.

name: Villa Rai Restaurant
address: Гърмен BG, общ, ул. Горни Бани 8, 2947 Ognyanovo, Bulgaria
text: Big portions, quality food for price. Good atmosphere.

name: Винарна "Златен Рожен" (Zlaten Rozhen Winery)
text: Delicious wines, good price for the quality

name: NICO LAZARIDI Château Nico Lazaridi - Οινοποιείο Νίκου Λαζαρίδη
text: Really good tour of winery and good wine tastings. Prices are a little bit expensive but rather than that good quality

name: Nefeli Hotel (Αλεξανδρουπολη)
address: 5 Th Klm National Road, Komotinis, Alexandroupoli 681 00, Greece
text: Old hotel, definitely not a 4 star quality

name: ÖZ-EN ET LOKANTASI
text: A flavor that hasn't lost its taste in years. The restaurant that comes to mind when you say satir kebab. Their buffalo yogurt is also great.

name: Villa Melnik
text: Awesome wine tour and tasting. Lady managing the tasting was really kind. Good quality of wines. Recommending to all!

name: Tokaj Wine Bar
text:
Really traditional bar, good design.
They do not speak English well but you figure it out somehow to order :)
Enjoyed the wines. Good place for tasting.

name: MrAlkohol Drink Shop
text: Awesome selection and quantity of wines & liquors!!

name: Drop Shop Wine Bar
text: Bar is only open after 5 PM. Before that, only bottle sales available

name: Fat Mama
text:
Good junk foods. Nice plates to share.
Too much food smell inside.

name: Ikonik Parlament
text: Nice designed hotel, good complementary coffee. Toilets in the rooms are a little bit weird, only glass door, lack of privacy. Heater does not work.

name: Fittbutiq Bistro
text: No breakfast after 11:30 AM.

name: KOOOL
address: fszt, Budapest, Szent István krt. 21, 1055 Hungary
text:
Didn't enjoy ginger shot.
Good food.

name: SixLetter Coffee Co. - Specialty Coffee Bar & Breakfast, Brunch
text: Good eggs benedict and pistachio croissant

name: Huncutka Bite and Wine
text: Toooo natural wines as glass. If you are not into that, i do not recommend to try.

name: Brunch & Lunch
text:
Best healthy brownie ever.
Good breakfast

name: Instant-Fogas Complex
text: Enter the place before 11 pm or you will wait a long line

name: Szimpla Kert
text:
Enter the place before 7 pm or you will wait for a huge line afterwards.

Do not see any room to sell food

name: Tasting Table Budapest (Wine Shop)
text: If you make a wine tasting, you need to make a reservation before. It is until 6 pm. However, you can walk in for a wine flight until 8 pm. Good selection of wines and really helpful staff.

name: Castle Garden Bazaar
text: Awesome place, definitely worth to visit

name: Akru Urla
text:
Their pizzas and some of the appetizers we had were really good, but despite the pasta being fresh and tasty ingredient-wise, it wasn't that enjoyable due to the lack of sauce. Pizza is the way to go for food here.

The by-the-glass wine served was good. The staff is very polite.

name: Champion Wine Vinarium&Gastro Boutique
text: The best tasting place in town. I wish I tried this place in my first day so all days I could have visit here. Perfect wine options, good design, lovely owners. Just amazing!!!

name: DeliGibi Şarküteri
text: The lamb lokum was really good, burgers were decent too.

name: Cup N Cake Urla
address: Camiatik, Tatar Cami Sk. No: 5, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: Their ice cream was pretty sugary. Gets sickening if you have too much.

name: Malgaca Dondurmacısı
address: Camiatik, Tatar Cami Sk. no:14, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: They have really high quality ice cream but the more unusual-looking ones felt a bit forced :) sea salt caramel became our favorite. Plain fruity ones like lemon and peach are also great. The ones with rose geranium and basil were a bit disappointing.

name: Rumeli Patisserie
text: Their ice cream was really good. Dark chocolate, pistachio, and honey almond were great.

name: Villa Veria
text: A clean and nice hotel for the price.

name: Çeşme Açık Hava Tiyatrosu
text:
Nice stage but definitely don't buy E block seats.

Other than that, everyone is puffing away on cigarettes.

name: Castro Rooms Chios
text: Just wonderful hotel. We loved it! Good location, clean and big rooms, lovely staff.

name: The North East Wall of Chios Castle
text: Good view point for both sea view and also castle walls

name: Ottoman Bath in the Castle of Chios
text: Really worth to visit, one of the few well protected historical places in Chios.

name: DENIZ TOURS
text: Tours are nice but unfortunately a bit too rushed. We couldn't spend nearly enough time in Pyrgi and Mesta. Apparently they don't take you to Mavra Volia anymore either. Wish they had more extended tour options for staying guests, aside from the ones rushing to catch the day-trip ferry.

name: Byzantine Museum of Chios (Mecidiye Mosque)
text: Only good to see from outside. Entrance price is 4 eur per person and it is not worth it at all. They only have descriptions in Greek and they do not bother to say it before you get the tickets.

name: Pura Vida
text: Too slow service. We had a breakfast plate and it was not good unfortunately.

name: 44 Bar · Coffee Shop
text: Good wine options as glass. Good music and atmosphere!

name: Sueño Coffee-Bar
text: Good breakfast! Omelette and fried eggs were wonderful

name: Mpoukia Mpoukia
text:
Food was not too good at overall. They overcooked octopus, it was really hard to chew. Salad and saganaki was ok. Housewine is good though. Expensive comparing to quality.

Waiter does not know to speak English.

name: Glaroi Beach
text:
Sea looks beautiful but a little bit cold. Directly deep. Karfas could be a better option for the ones who with child.

2 eur sunbed price per person + a water complimentary. If you enjoy cold water, place is really nice. Do not expect good food from the kitchen though.

name: Basilique du Sacré-Cœur de Montmartre
address: 35 Rue du Chevalier de la Barre, 75018 Paris, France
text: Perfect panoramic view. 7 eur each person, but 290 steps. Really tiring but definitely worth it

name: Jardins du Trocadéro
address: Pl. du Trocadéro et du 11 Novembre, 75016 Paris, France
text: Best place to view Eiffel Tower

name: Ladurée
text: Good desserts :)

name: Librairie Auguste Blaizot
address: 164 Rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris, France
text: A library to be in love. Just wonderful ancient old books. Employees were really helpful too.

name: BOWL UP healthy food
text: Nice food! They only have menu in French which is a problem. Rather than that, staff is helpful, delicious and healthy.

name: Arc de Triomphe
address: Pl. Charles de Gaulle, 75008 Paris, France

name: Il était une oie dans le Sud Ouest
text: Traditional French restaurant. Duck leg was awesome. Other duck appetizers were ok

name: Perotti Franck
text: Good quiche and creme de brulee

name: Librairie Saint Ferdinand
text: Only religious books

name: WINESS
text: Nice store! After we asked what is pastis liquor like, owner let us taste a little bit! :)

name: Le Lutin
text: Really friendly staff

name: L'Entre deux Vins
text: Nice wine store. You can have 3 different reds and whites per glass per day. We also had chance to try of cassis liquor. Owner is really helpful.

name: Le Sens Unique
text: Good ambiance, glass wines are good. Awesome food, duck leg was perfect.

name: MAM par Stéphanie Le Quellec - Fourcroy
text: Awesome pecan cookies!

name: Truffes Folies
text: Awesome place to eat! A little bit expensive but all worth it. Soup and ravioli was our favorite.

name: Le XXV
address: 225 Rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris, France
text: Too long lines. Employee was really quick but not enough for this crowd. Quiches were good but a little bit expensive.

name: Le P'tit Wagram
text: Crispy pesto gouda was just awesome. Onion soup was ok. Selection of wines are good but served a little bit more warm.

name: Jolis Mômes
text: Nice bar, good selection of white wines, nice appetizers such as truffle gouda

name: Boulangerie Pâtisserie Sylvain Marie
text: Awesome quiches! Best in town. Cake with pistachio was not good. Staff is a little bit slow.

name: Hotel Elysees Flaubert Paris
text:
Location is good but anything than that is not good. Old hotel, all facilities are old and neglected. Rooms are little. Sound isolation is 0.

Not worth for the price.

name: Kaklık Mağarası ve Yüzme Havuzu
text: Absolutely a must-see beauty. The smell is awful when you first enter but you get used to it inside. Definitely wear slippers, the floors are very wet.

name: Ulubey Canyon Nature Park
text: The setting is absolutely stunning. The cafe-restaurant is cheap, the viewpoint area is enjoyable. The only concern is there's no limit on how many people they let in, which didn't inspire confidence. It can get especially crowded during holidays. When you go all the way to the edge you feel quite a bit of shaking. Entry was 15 TL as of July 2023.

name: 180 derece Fırın Pastane Çandarlı
address: Çandarlı, sahil plaj caddesi no:43/B, 35980 Dikili/İzmir
text: Their cakes are good, nice atmosphere. The plain lemonade was a bit too sweet (despite them calling it sugar-free) but the strawberry lemonade was good.

name: Çandarlı Castle
text: Looks nice from the outside. It was closed for the holiday so we couldn't see inside.

name: Izmir Archaeological Museum
address: Bahri Baba Parkı, Konak Mahallesi, Halil Rıfat Paşa Cd. No:4, 35260 Konak/İzmir
text: Content is very weak. They don't even run the AC in July heat. Both the building and the content have been left neglected. Really weak compared to similar museums in Istanbul and Ankara. Truly sad.

name: Maritime Museum
text: The tour takes about 1 hour, guided tours run every 20 minutes. The content is great, as of July 2023 adult admission is 60 TL. Absolutely a must-see museum.

name: Kızlarağası Inn
address: Kızlarağası Hanı Hanı 871 Sokak No:19/106 Hisarönü, 35250 Konak/İzmir
text: Absolutely a must-visit place.

name: Menemenci Alaattin
text: Home-style food isn't bad, prices aren't too expensive. The place isn't the most hygienic though.

name: Çiya Sofrası
text: Food is great, prices are fair, staff are attentive. The sun-dried tomato bean fritters were superb.

name: AYUMİ SUSHİ BAR
address: cevizli mahallesi zuhal caddesi ritim istanbul avm, no 46, 34846 Maltepe/İstanbul
text: We ordered delivery, food arrived late but it was hot. Portions aren't very big but the taste is good.

name: Soho House Istanbul
address: Evliya Çelebi, Meşrutiyet Cd. No:56, 34430 Beyoğlu/İstanbul
text: Unnecessary pretentiousness, the staff literally think they're something special. I've never seen a place with such rude reception, so disrespectful.

name: Taverna Kostas
text: Great food. Swordfish, grilled octopus and salad was awesome. Owners are quite good persons :)

name: Decan Bistro and 7 Grams Breakfast Brunch (same place)
text: Good for breakfast. Healthy options.

name: Street of the Knights of Rhodes
text: Wonderful

name: Palace of the Grand Master of the Knights of Rhodes
text: One of the best castles I have ever seen

name: Vintage
text: Good variety of wines store but a little bit expensive

name: Viewpoint Western Shoreline and City
text: Good view point

name: St Stephen's Hill (Monte Smith)
text: Awesome view point

name: Acropolis of Rhodes
text: Not worth to visit at all.

name: TurgutReis Balık Marmaris
address: Armutalan, Yunus Nadi Cd. No:116, 48700 Marmaris/Muğla
text: Quality seafood, portions are filling.

name: Benito’s Tabacco Farm
text: Just an awesome place. Benito is a really cool guy. Really liked the farm.

name: Mural of Prehistory
text: Awesome ambiance

name: Restaurant Mural de la Prehistoria
text: Pina colada is just the best in the country

name: Restaurante Rancho San Vicente
text: Chicken and potato is really nice, good music

name: Indian Cave
text: Perfect ambiance!

name: Legendarios del Guajirito Buena Vista Social Club
text: Best entertainment in Cuba ever. 35 usd entrance within 3 drinks included without meal. Definitely worth it. Need reservation before because it is full.

name: El Floridita Bar
text: Daiquiri is really the best

name: Ivan Chef Justo
address: 4JRV+9H2 La Esquina, Aguacate, La Habana, Cuba
text: Too expensive comparing to Cuba standards

name: Hotel Inglaterra
address: 416 P.º de Martí, La Habana, Cuba
text: About restaurant: good food, live music

name: Azotea 1940
text: They just called us for food to upstairs, but after we were there, they told kitchen is open but nothing exists as a meal

name: Mirador de Bacunayagua
address: Mirador Puente de Bacunayagua, Bacunayagua, Cuba
text: Good view, original pina colada

name: Brisas del Caribe
text: Beach was awesome but that was it. Food was just disgusting, all inclusive hotel but they do not have mojito, pina colada as cocktails. Even their options of alcohol are worst. Not worth it at all.

name: Che Guevara Mausoleum
address: Memorial Emesto Che Guevara, Santa Clara, Cuba
text: Well designed mausoleum. Emotional.

name: Los Caneyes
text: They have open buffet but food is ok.

name: Dönerci Vedat Usta | İzmir - Altındağ
text: Really delicious doner place, portions are filling.

name: Il Trionfo
text:
Place is well designed and food is fast however not a quite italian restaurant at all :) if they change their concept to another, they would be more successful. Everything is too salty.

Only italian restaurant in the world which does not have a wine list at the menu.

name: Restaurante La Nueva Era
text:
Place is really well decorated, nice food. However too slow service. Seafood pasta was worth to try.

Live music was just perfect!

name: Taberna La Canchánchara
address: 90 C. Real del Jigüe, Trinidad 62600, Cuba
text: Canchanchara is one of the best cocktails I drank in Cuba. It was just awesome!!!

name: Bar Doña Leonor
text: Best pina colada in town!!!

name: Disco Ayala
text: Really nice place to definitely see. Original disco in cave. Inside is really hot however, not enough air conditioning.

name: Ranchón Ancón
text: Food is really good, big portions, especially seafood is awesome. Coconut was really nice. However the facility is really awful. Toilets are really dirty, no shower at the beach.

name: Arkas Sanat Urla
text: It's great that you can visit for free, and the artworks inside are definitely worth seeing.

name: Bizim Et Galerisi Kasap Fuat
text: Meat quality is great, food is very well done.

name: Bozcaada Vasil Coffee&Bakery
text:
Supposedly a place with gluten-free options, but since the season hasn't started yet they don't have any gluten-free food.

Service is very slow, and the food portions are small. Prices are expensive for the portions.

name: Hasan Tefik Restoran ve Zeytinyağı
text: Nice atmosphere, quality food. The octopus was delicious. The sardine stuffed grape leaves were truly a real specialty.

name: Bozcaadalı VeliDede
address: Alaybey Mah. Cumhuriyet Meydanı No:9, Alaybey, Çınarlı Çarşı Cd. No:55, 17680 Bozcaada/Çanakkale
text: Great products, they have gluten-free options too. Their mastic coffee is tasty, and the pistachio coconut one is amazing :)

name: Bozcaada Castle
text: Looks really beautiful from the outside.

name: Tenedion Winehouse
text:
You can only try Camlibag wines. A tasting of 9 wines at 5 cl each is 150 TL. Price-wise it's really affordable but the quality of the wines is truly mediocre, like they've turned to vinegar. Before committing to the 9-wine tasting, it's worth trying just 1 first to see if it appeals to your palate. Even though the grapes change, the style stays the same.

Other than that, the place feels a bit abandoned, and the people doing the explanations aren't very knowledgeable about wine. They seemed like they were doing you a favor by being there.

name: Ataol Saraplari
address: Alaybey, Çınar Çeşme Sk. Nu:3, 17680 Bozcaada/Çanakkale
text: Nice setting, the staff are pleasant to chat with, but the wines are mediocre. You can try 5 cl for 30 TL. Worth trying to see the island style, but definitely taste one before ordering more to see if it suits your palate.

name: Ayapetro Otel
address: No:16,, Alaybey, Hacı Hasan Sk., 17680 Bozcaada/Çanakkale
text:
Way more quality than what you pay for. The owners are incredibly attentive. Beautifully designed, breakfast is nice.

Our only negative comment is that since the room insulation is poor, you hear a lot of noise from other rooms and the hallway, and there's a bit of smell coming from the toilet drains. But I think these are problems common to all the old houses on the island in general.

name: Amadeus Sarapcilik / Winery
text: By far the best winery on Bozcaada. For 30 TL you can taste 5 cl from all their bottles. The grape varieties, especially the whites, are very original. Absolutely a must-visit.

name: Tomas Terry Theater
text: Old and historical theatre. Worth to visit.

name: Club Cienfuegos
text: Nice sea view but food was ok.
Red snapper, lobster, seafood paella was perfect. Just do not forget to ask the food with less salt. Long service waiting time unfortunately.

name: La Bodeguita Del Medio
text: Cool place, music was good. Not the best mojito in town. Meals are ok.

name: Atatürk statue
text: Can't pass by without paying respects :)

name: Castle of the Three Kings of Morro
text: Please stop by their souvenir shop. Really nice people-souvenirs as well as good prices.

name: Parque Miramar
text: Just a wonderful place. Beautiful ficus trees.

name: Hotel Vedado 500
address: calle 17 entre l y m vedado, Cuba
text: Dirty sheets/towels, definitely bring with you. Hot water is low, internet problems always.

name: Gran Café "El Louvre"
address: 4JPR+W7J, Blvd. de San Rafael, La Habana, Cuba
text: Music is perfect. Drink prices are good. Food is ok.

name: Don Eduardo Alegre
text: Seafood pasta was really good. However place is a little bit expensive, especially drinks.

name: El Castillo
text:
Well worth world wonder. Just amazing temple.

Really hot area, bringing umbrella makes sense.

name: Ik Kil Cenote
address: Km. 122, Valladolid - Merida Supermanzana Tablaje Catastral 510, 97753 X-Calakoop, Yuc., Mexico
text: Just fantastic.

name: Coco Bongo Cancun
address: Blvd. Kukulcan 9.5, Punta Cancun, Zona Hotelera, 77500 Cancún, Q.R., Mexico
text: Really crowded but perfect shows!! Really worth it for the $$$

name: Cotorritos
address: Blvd. Kukulcan Km. 9.5, Punta Cancun, Zona Hotelera, 77500 Cancún, Q.R., Mexico
text:
Do not order cocktail shrimps :)

Tacos are really small. Expensive comparing to quality

name: Súper Chedraui Selecto
address: Boulevard Kukulcan mz. 48, LT. ZC1 - F1 Y 6A, Punta Cancun, Zona Hotelera, 77500 Cancún, Q.R., Mexico
text: Perfect for booze and also souvenirs

name: ibis Cancun Centro
address: Ave Tulum Y Nichupte, Supermanzana 11 Manzana 2 L3, 77504 Cancún, Q.R., Mexico
text: Small and smelly rooms. Not too close to the sea coast. Breakfast is not too good.

name: Piazza San Marco
address: P.za San Marco, 30100 Venezia VE, Italy
text: One of the best squares ever

name: Arena di Verona
text: Good view of city. Ancient landmark. Cannot compare with colosseum but still nice to see.

name: Duomo di Milano
address: P.za del Duomo, 20122 Milano MI, Italy
text: Best cathedral ever.

name: Al Canton

name: Pasticceria Castelvecchio

name: Berberè Pizzeria

name: Hotel Sanpi Milano
address: Via Lazzaro Palazzi, 18, 20124 Milano MI, Italy
text: Good location. Hotel is OK but some halls are designed weirdly.

name: Elk Bakery - The Garden
text: Good and healthy breakfast.

name: VIVAVINO WINE SHOP
address: Via Guglielmo Oberdan, 18, 37121 Verona VR, Italy
text: Good shop! Really nice and different stuff about wines.

name: Yguana Cafè
address: Via Papa Gregorio XIV, 16, 20123 Milano MI, Italy
text: Best aperitivo place ever. 1 drink for 13€ which includes all you can eat for the night. Really delicious food.

name: Quzu Ocakbaşı
address: Bostanlı, Şht. Cengiz Topel Cd. 26 A, 35590 Karşıyaka/İzmir

name: Arkas Sanat Alsancak
text: Definitely worth a visit, there are some really beautiful works.

name: Ataturk Museum
address: Alsancak Mh.,, Gündüğdu Caddesi No:242, 35220 Konak/İzmir

name: Meşhur Hisarönü Şambalicisi 1942'den Beri

name: Osman Usta Antalya Borekcisi
text: Perfect flavor.

name: Orkinos Restaurant
address: Venüs Apt. Küçükyalı, Çankaya Mahallesi, Göztepe, M.Kemal Sahil Yolu Bulvarı No:437/A, 35290 Konak/İzmir

name: Zenginoğlu Turşu
address: Karabaş, Akça Cami Cd. No:89 D:79, 41040 İzmit/Kocaeli
text: They have really unique pickles like peach, corn, medlar. They have everything. Absolutely a must-stop.

name: Balıkçım Balık Pişiricisi Tuncay'ın Yeri
address: Atatürk, Abdi İpekçi Cd. No:41, 09200 Söke/Aydın
text: Both the fish and the seafood are great. Our favorite was the sole fish and fried mussels.

name: Arctic SnowHotel & Glass Igloos
text: Good for the touristic visit. No need to go to the far hotels if you are in the Rovaniemi area.

name: Alko Rovaniemi keskusta
text: Sametti blueberry wine should definitely be tried :)

name: Santa's Salmon Place
address: Santa Claus Village, Pukinpolku 5, 96930 Rovaniemi, Finland
text: Awesome meal. The taste of the meal is really nice. Wood fire is really good for cooking the salmons. You need to wait in line since the place is really small.

name: Santa Claus Office
text: Most overrated place at the area, at least 30 mins waiting period for a photo with official santa :))

name: Arctic Circle Husky Park
text: Conditions of the huskies do not seem very good. Also some of them are a little aggressive. Not soo good in terms of park too. Sad for those poor huskies.

name: Santa's Motor Park - Arctic Circle
text:
Ice karting, awesome experience! Definitely worth the $$.

Do not forget to bring thick gloves as your hands are going to freeze :)

name: Rovaniemi Airport
text: No freeshop section on the arrival area so it is wise to buy some stuff from departure airport

name: Apukka Resort - Glass Igloos Hotel - Husky & Reindeer Farm and Tours
text:
Really nice and fancy hotel. View from aurora rooms at the open sky is awesome. However everything in the hotel is within as paid service (such as aurora wake up l, late check out etc) so be aware.

Paid activities are really nice but I think hotel is lack of free activities at all.

For paid activities we horse riding, snowmobile, fin sauna carriage. All of them were great, we can recommend.

name: Restaurant Aitta
text: Open buffet is really great and paying for its $$$. However a la carte menu is really expensive comparing to the buffet as well as limited. Definitely should go for buffet.

name: Mopaş
address: Adatepe Mah, Şht. Hikmet Alp Cd. No:36, 34840 Maltepe/İstanbul
text: The produce section is very fresh. A grocery store where you can find everything you're looking for.

name: Sante Wine & More
address: Akat, Haydar Aliyev Cd. No. 2C, 34335 Beşiktaş/İstanbul
text: You should definitely stop by for their great wine selection.

name: Ada Manolya
address: Feyzullah, Bağdat Cad. No:254 D:E, 34843 Maltepe/İstanbul
text: Really delicious cakes.

name: Rossi Suadiye
address: Suadiye, Plaj Yolu Sk. No:23, 34740 Kadıköy/İstanbul
text: The food is really great, gotta give credit where it's due, but especially the wine menu is unnecessarily expensive for the wines they serve. The house wine they offer is just a bottled wine from another brand. The food is superb but they should make a few adjustments on the wine side.

name: Craft Beer Lab (Caddebostan)
address: Caddebostan, Bağdat Cad. No:290 D:A, 34728 Kadıköy/İstanbul
text: Nice atmosphere. Good variety of beers and a rich food menu.

name: Gordias Şarapçılık, şarap evi
text: You can visit for a tasting with a reservation in advance. They don't serve tasting glasses, you need to open a bottle. Prices are reasonable, the wine tastes good. The setting is especially mystical in winter. Worth stopping by even just for a chat with Mrs. Canan.

name: Kuntra Wine Bar
text:
The wine menu is great but the food quality could be improved.

On our second visit the food was much better. In fact, with their current prices it can now truly be called a great value-for-money spot.

name: Midas Tümülüsü
text: Unfortunately they don't let people inside the tumulus anymore because visitors were causing damage. You can only peek through the door. Shout-out to the craftsmen who carved their names by the door when it was opened in 1963.

name: Gordion Museum
text: The museum content is really good but the signage and building are weak. Could use better maintenance, needs an overhaul.

name: Gordion Ancient City
text: Unfortunately it hasn't been restored much. Don't go with very high expectations given its current state.

name: AOÇ Lagania
text: Good kokoreç.

name: Atakule
text: Nice city view.

name: Tulumtas Cave
text: Absolutely an incredible cave but it looks like it'll fall victim to the houses built on top of it. Cave work has been halted because it puts those houses at risk. According to the security guard, it won't reopen again. I hope that's not the case.

name: Türk Tarih Müzesi ve Parkı
text: The statues and the surroundings outside look great, but putting such a basic museum in such a huge building was quite a disappointment. The illustrations are nice but content-wise it's close to zero unfortunately, a waste of the building they invested in.

name: alma table
text: The ambiance is really nice, the food is genuinely well done and worth its price. The wine menu could be enriched a bit more and wines better matched to the dishes.

name: Republic Museum (The Second Parliament Building)
address: Doğanbey Mahallesi, Cumhuriyet Cd. No:7, 06050 Altındağ/Ankara
text: An incredibly meaningful and emotional building and museum. Definitely a place every citizen should see.

name: Museum of Anatolian Civilizations
text: Amazing museum, great exhibits.

name: Kemankeşan Kahve
text: They serve traditional Turkish coffees. We tried the Kemankesan, Assyrian, and mastic dibek coffees and found them pretty good. Their moist cakes were also great.

name: Ankara Castle
text:
There's a parking spot somewhere inside the castle, we found a space by luck but it can be full especially during busy times. Parking below and walking up might make more sense.

Other than that, the interior of the castle and the views are quite beautiful. I wasn't expecting such a large area. You should definitely go and spend plenty of time at the museums there too. You could easily dedicate 2-3 days total.

name: Avlu Urla Bistro & Bar & Patisserie
text: A really fun place with live music! Especially the band Narkoz that plays every Friday is awesome.

name: TCDD 3. Bölge Müd Otoparkı
address: Umurbey, TCDD 3. Bölge Md. Otoparkı, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text: Nice parking lot, usually has spots available, easy to get in and out.

name: Alsancak Punta Katlı Otoparkı
text: Going up and down is very narrow and you keep turning constantly. I'd recommend trying the TCDD open parking lot just ahead instead. They had space while this one was completely full.

name: Sade Urla
text:
This turned out to be a restaurant where we went without any expectations and ate amazing food. The adabeyi kavurma, flavor bomb, and artichoke shrimp hot starters were superb. We were also really happy with the parsley and beet dishes we ordered as meze-salad. Overall the flavor of everything we ate was top-notch. Price-wise it's a bit expensive unfortunately so it's not a value spot, but it's a great choice for a quality meal.

After our second visit we can say the same things. The food is truly amazingly delicious and worth every penny.

name: Gia Urla Restoran
text:
The food is absolutely superb, the ambiance is very well done. There are minor hiccups in service but the food is so good you don't even notice. The head waiter recommended we go heavy on the meats, and that's what we did, no regrets at all. Absolutely recommended.

December 2022 edit: unfortunately the chef has changed, and with that prices have gone way up while portions have shrunk incredibly. Flavor-wise it's still okay but in its current state you're definitely not getting your money's worth.

name: Delirium Café
address: Imp. de la Fidélité 4, 1000 Bruxelles, Belgium
text: Nice for hanging out, both quiet and loud rooms exist. Really big place.

name: Jeanneke Pis
address: Imp. de la Fidélité 10-12, 1000 Bruxelles, Belgium
text: Most overrated symbol v2

name: ibis Brussels off Grand Place
address: Rue du Marché aux Herbes 100, 1000 Bruxelles, Belgium
text: A really nice city hotel. Perfect location.

name: Natural Corner
text: Do not have good variety

name: Manneken Pis
text: Most overrated place and symbol of the city ever

name: Wolf food market
address: Rue du Fossé aux Loups 50, 1000 Bruxelles, Belgium
text: Lots of different options. Good beer and ambiance.

name: Bruges Beer Experience
text: Place was too crowded as we couldn't make it to visit the museum. For bar experience, while tasting environment is nice, we didn't like the beers they are serving. Better places can be found.

name: Le Pain Quotidien
text: Good for breakfast. Especially for crowded groups it is really good because most of the places are really little.

name: Saint Bavo's Cathedral
text: A must see in Gent

name: Graffiti Street
text: Not worth visiting

name: Dulle Griet
text: Awesome bar! Definitely go for the long beers for a great experience :)

name: Bar Terroir
text: Selection of wines are not too good unfortunately.

name: Julie's House - taartenhuis
text: Good for desserts.

name: Castle of the Counts
text: Awesome castle! It was free during christmasmarket on Friday night.

name: Boutique Hotel de Flandre
text: Location is good, also a lot of good service and complimentary such as waters a bottle of wine. However sound isolation between rooms are really bad. You can wake up at 6 when the person on the upper room walks to the WC..

name: Ristorante Pizzeria Venedik
address: 1382 Sokak, Kültür, Alsancak - İzmir Gül sokak No:10/A-B, 35220 Konak
text: Amazing flavor, all their pizzas are excellent. Since they don't use a wood fire there's no smoky taste, which actually makes the pizza even better. Their homemade house wines are also delicious. The best pizza place in Izmir, consistently great for years.

name: Quinta do Bomfim
text: Perfect view. Good wines. Glass wines are a little bit expensive comparing to the bottles but they really have good variety of wines.

name: Rufete
address: R. António Manuel Saraiva 13A, 5085-037 Pinhão, Portugal
text: Nice traditional Portuguese food!

name: Quinta do Beijo, Sociedade Agrícola e Comercial Lda
address: R. de São Bento No 1, 5060-011, Portugal
text: Really good wines, also perfect winery tour!

name: Quinta Val Moreira
address: Quinta do Val Moreira – Marmelal – Sto Adrião, 5110-672, Portugal
text: Good wines, good winery tour!

name: Douro Valley Wine Tours
address: R. das Mimosas 4510, 4510-607 São Pedro da Cova, Portugal
text: Awesome tour company for Douro!

name: Igreja do Carmo
text: Amazing building

name: Mercado Ferreira Borges
text: Visited at the weekend and good designs exist for sale such as tshirts, wooden jobs etc. really liked the stuff.

name: Casa do Infante
text: Not too much interesting stuff

name: Luís I Bridge
text: Watching sunset from the bridge is priceless

name: Espaço Porto Cruz
address: Largo Miguel Bombarda 23, 4400-222 Vila Nova de Gaia, Portugal
text: Tasting for regular wines is nice but the port wines served in tasting are not too good quality

name: Church of Saint Ildefonso
address: Praça da Batalha s/n, 4000-101 Porto, Portugal
text: Entrance not needed in my opinion, only looking from just the entrance would be enough.

name: Café Santiago
address: R. de Passos Manuel 226, 4000-382 Porto, Portugal
text:
Best dinner ever! Really enjoyed the quality of Francesinha.
Also the waiter knows nearly all languages :)

name: Chapel of Souls
address: R. de Santa Catarina 428, 4000-444 Porto, Portugal
text: Perfect structure!

name: Livraria Lello
text: Tickets definitely needed for entrance but you can also buy them while waiting on the line. At least 15-20 minutes waiting in the line happening. Place is so crowded. However still an original place to definitely worth visiting.

name: Hard Rock Cafe
text: Comparing the prices of other similar restaurants in Porto, this is way more expensive. Shrimps are perfect as always.

name: NOLA KITCHEN
address: Praça D. Filipa de Lencastre 25, 4050-259 Porto, Portugal
text:
Good place to have breakfast. Menu is completely different than regular ones, but worth to try.

Inside of the restaurant smells a lot of food so outside is a much wiser choice.

name: Ris8tto da Baixa
text: Awesome food. Both pesto and mushroom risotto ones really perfect. Dish seems a little bit small at first but definitely fulfilling. Wine selection is also nice.

name: The Editory Boulevard Aliados Hotel - Preferred Hotels
text: Rooms were a little bit small but super functional. Really enjoyed the complimentary port wine, chestnuts and apples. Location is super, really in the center. Design of the hotel is original but sometimes it is a little bit creepy :)

name: Quinta da Regaleira
text: Wonderful place.

name: Initiation Well
address: Quinta da Regaleira, R. Barbosa du Bocage 9, 2710-567 Sintra, Portugal
text: Just a wonderful place. Amazing. One time experience. Do not miss it if you are visiting Lisbon.

name: National Palace of Pena
text: Wonderful. It was not too crowded on weekdays at afternoon November. Buying tickets makes sense for not waiting in the lines.

name: Refúgio do Ciclista
text: Good food! We had mixed meat plate and it was more than expected!

name: National Pantheon
address: Campo de Santa Clara, 1100-471 Lisboa, Portugal
text: Great building

name: Church of St. Vincent de Fora
address: Largo de São Vicente, 1100-472 Lisboa, Portugal
text: Seems nice for visiting

name: Park and Palace of Monserrate
address: R. Barbosa du Bocage 136, 2710-405 Sintra, Portugal
text: Unbelievable place. Palace is not the greatest but the garden is. All the trees etc just awesome. Definitely from another world.

name: Maria Catita
text: Really nice food! We got octopus, tuna tapas, lobster-shellfish and prawns and all of them were really nice. Wine selections for glass were also ok. Definitely worth to try.

name: Lisbon Cathedral
text: A nice one to see when passing by

name: Castelo de São Jorge
address: R. de Santa Cruz do Castelo, 1100-129 Lisboa, Portugal
text: Just an amazing place. A must see. We watched sunset in here and the view was awesome.

name: Carmo Archaeological Museum
text: A really unique place to see. Also museum is nice. Price is 5 eur which is ok comparing to the what they have inside.

name: Casa Portuguesa do Pastel de Bacalhau
text: A really different taste to experience. Personally it is not my style but still the presentation is really nice.

name: Ginginha do Carmo
address: Calçada do Carmo 37 A, 1200-090 Lisboa, Portugal
text: Awesome place to taste Ginginha! Serving lady was also super nice.

name: Rua Augusta Arch
text: Just an amazing place to see.

name: Garrafeira Nacional
text: We came here by just chance but actually had a chance to taste a 1878 port wine. Amazing!! Super recommending to check it out to everyone visiting.

name: Manteigaria
text: Nice natas!!

name: Limoncello
text: Good food. I had a limoncello salad and it was really both delicious and also satisfying.

name: Pastéis de Belém
text: Had some quite fews nata on the both Lisbon and Porto and this place does actually the best all over! An essential must to have in Lisbon.

name: Monument to the Discoveries
text: Just an amazing monument!

name: Taguscruises Boat Tours & Yacht Charter
address: Doca do Bom Sucesso, Avenida de Brasília, 1400-038 Lisboa, Portugal
text: Tour was really nice however the guys that took our group to the tour were a little bit too talky and had some intense jokes

name: Cooking Lisbon
text: Chef is really nice and the menu was also good. However it took a little bit longer than we thought so we were a little bit exhausted in the end. Additionally air condition system was not working very good I think because all of our clothes and even our hairs were smelling food afterwards.

name: Dom Pedro Lisboa Hotel, a member of Radisson Individuals
address: Av. Eng. Duarte Pacheco 24, 1070-110 Lisboa, Portugal
text: Rooms were big but air conditioner system is a mess. Hot was not working and it was cold in the rooms even if they changed the whole air condition. Also some food smells coming from there time to time. Breakfast was really nice.

name: Ninja Buns - Lisboa
text: Food was nice, wine choices were not too good.

name: Taberna Moderna
text: Really nice dishes! Service was also nice too.

name: Ünal Kardeşler Katmer Salonu
text: Nice place for breakfast, their katmer is good and not too greasy, the sigara börek wasn't bad either. Prices are reasonable.

name: Kokoreçci Asım Usta Göztepe
address: Mehmet Ali Akman, İnönü Cd. No:668A, 35290 Konak/İzmir
text: The kokorec is awesome but content-wise there's no need to order double meat. The single portions are already packed full. Half could be 1.5 portions max, and the doubles are way too much meat.

name: Hiç Lokanta - Urla
address: Sanat Sokağı, Yeni, Zafer Cd. No:52, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text:
Staff are very attentive and kind. For food we went with the fish, it was a bit heavy for us but definitely well-crafted with gourmet flavors. The fact that they offer all wines by the glass is excellent. Overall a successful restaurant, prices are a bit steep but compared to similar places it's worth it. The reason I'm taking off one star is that the tables are unfortunately way too close together. While eating it feels like you're sitting with the next table over, impossible to have a private conversation. If they could add a bit more space between tables it would be so much better.

Additional note after our second visit: we left even less satisfied this time. Some dishes, especially in an effort to be original, had random combinations that ruined the flavor. Since they're a sustainable restaurant they don't give out cologne-scented wet wipes, but they haven't come up with an alternative solution either :)

name: Urla Yosun Restaurant
address: Balıkçı limanı, İskele, 2121. Sk. no:6/A, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: Meze flavors are not bad, the best ones were the seafood noodles and beet spread.

name: Klazomenai Ancient City of Olive Presses
text: In its current state there's not too much worth seeing, but most likely within 5-10 years as excavations increase there'll be a lot more to see.

name: Kumrucu Şevki Waffle`cı Akın
text: Kumru flavor was average.

name: Nuvia Bostanlı Breakfast - Kitchen & Bar
address: Bostanlı, Bestekar Yusuf Nalkesen Sok. 2 /A, 35590 Karşıyaka/İzmir
text: We didn't try the breakfast but the hot appetizers and snacks to go with beer were average.
We stumbled upon this completely by chance. A totally unknown place but definitely very well preserved, with deep spiritual significance.

name: Çelebi Unlu Mamuller
text: They have original bomba pastries, good ingredient quality. The peanut one was nice.

name: Poka Coffee Roasters
address: Alsancak, Kıbrıs Şehitleri Cd. No:171, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text: Lezzet topu were well done.

name: Alsancak Dostlar Fırını
address: Alsancak, Kıbrıs Şehitleri Cd. No:120, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text: Amazing boyoz, incredible flavor. Especially the pastrami and spinach ones are superb.

name: Asrın Pide Kebap çorba pizza
address: Hacı İsa, Bülent Baratalı Blv. No: 72 D:1A, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: The food tastes great and prices are fair for the quality. A real bang-for-your-buck spot. We ordered delivery and since the distance was long it arrived a bit cold. Maybe they could use more insulated containers, but other than that we had no complaints.

name: Welldone Suadiye
address: Suadiye, Selim Ragıp Emeç Sk. No: 27, 34740 Kadıköy/İstanbul
text: Food is mediocre, the outdoor area completely reeks of cigarettes.

name: Beyaz Firin
text: Ideal for families with kids.

name: Mums Homestay & Apartments- A Real Homestay Experience
address: Nagarjune-1, Radhakrishna Temple, 6 Manakamanamarga, Street, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
text: Good ambience, nice food!
From a touristical perspective, not too much difference comparing to other temples in the city center. So you can skip this one if you are only a tourist.

name: Shakya Siddhartha Thanka Painting School
text: Do not buy any paintings before seeing here. They give the same ones half cheap prices comparing to Kathmandu center ones. Master paintings are super nice and very well detailed. Amazing work.

name: MANAKAMANA HANDICRAFTS & SINGING BOWL HOUSE
text: Best bowls in the town! Also one of the cheapest. Do not buy a bowl without talking with here.

name: Hard Rock Cafe Kathmandu
address: Hard Rock Cafe, Sherpa Mall, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
text: Same ambiance within other hard rock cafe’s all around the world as well as same menu. Good restaurant above Kathmandu standards.

name: Neydo Tashi Pharping Monastery
text: Super beautiful ambience. Really good monastery with awesome view and atmosphere. A must visit. If you can find a chance to see a ceremony, then definitely a must to do also!

name: Dakshinkali Temple
text: No need to go as touristical visit unless you are a believer. All place is trash and dirty, also bloody. Worst experience in Kathmandu.
One of the best places I see in Kathmandu. Really underrated and overlooked temple. A must visit when coming to Pharping.
Really nice, worth to visit!

name: Chilancho Stupa
text: Cannot compare within the other stupas but if you have time in town and want to see new places, you can visit

name: Orient Cafe
text: Really nice restaurant. Good food!

name: Swayambhu Mahachaitya
text: If possible, try to visit when the weather is not cloudy because view seems great.

name: Patan Darbar Square
text: Bhaktapur and Patan durbar squares a must visit in Kathmandu.

name: Pashupatinath Temple
text: This one has an unique atmosphere. Must go in the late afternoon or evening, far better experience within the darkness.
A good view of stupa, meals are also ok but if you go in high hours you can wait for a long time. Had our dinner in at least 2 hours

name: Buddha Stupa
text: One of the best touristical places that should be seen in Kathmandu.

name: Nyatapola Temple
text: One of the best temples in any of the durbars.

name: Bhaktapur Durbar Square
text: Far more cleaner and a better square, especially comparing to Kathmandu one. Definitely worth visiting. Also gift shops are much cheaper comparing to the center.

name: Northfield Cafe and Jesse James Bar
text: A nice restaurant. Cheese balls were amazing. Toilets are a little bit dirty.

name: Kathmandu's Beni Handicrafts
text: This one definitely needs more attention. Perfect volunteer work, all of the stuff made from recycled stuff via young Nepalese women which are supported from this shop.

name: Kathmandu Durbar Square
text: Comparing to other durbar squares, this one is way more chaotic. Too many street sellers which annoys after some period of time.

name: Tribhuvan International Airport
text: Too chaotic, super slow staff. If you have international flights, definitely a good advice to come before 3 hours of it.

name: Aloft Kathmandu Thamel
address: Chhaya Devi Complex, Amrit Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
text: Really nice hotel, far better comparing to the standards of Kathmandu. Elevators are a little bit problematic + too much food smell due to lack of AC on restaurant floor, otherwise no problems at all.

name: Favino Alsancak
address: Kültür Mahallesi, Alsancak, Dr. Mustafa Enver Bey Cd. No:11, 35220 Konak/İzmir
text: Wine selection is solid but the food is average.

name: Wine House Urla
text: The food was good, the venue is spacious, and the waiters are very attentive. Their wine list is impressive. Given today's prices, they haven't hiked theirs too much yet, so I'd say it's good value for money.

name: Denizyıldızı
text: The sea was really beautiful in August. Service-wise they're super fast too, makes sense to go when it's not crowded.

name: BJ's Restaurant & Brewhouse
address: 10690 N De Anza Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, United States
text: Good pizzas!

name: Foreign Cinema
address: 2534 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94110, United States
text:
Movie staging is just a background and a show, if you really want to watch it, it is not possible for both sound and light. No chance to hear anything. Maybe a subtitle could be a nice idea.

Food is good but portions are a little small.

name: Charmaine’s
address: Proper Hotel (Rooftop, 1100 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94102, United States
text: A good rooftop to chill. Hot cocktails are also nice, especially in a windy evening :)

name: Café de la Presse
address: 352 Grant Ave, San Francisco, CA 94108, United States
text: Nice onion soup!

name: EPIC Steak
address: 369 The Embarcadero, San Francisco, CA 94105, United States
text: Best ribeye ever tasted, really epic :) but also expensive.

name: Boudin Bakery
address: 160 Jefferson St, San Francisco, CA 94133, United States
text: Really nice soups, beef chili one was not so good but clam one was awesome!

name: Sea Lion Viewing Area
address: Pier 39, San Francisco, CA 94133, United States
text: One of the best areas in town :) awesome sea lions.

name: Alcatraz Island
text: Whole island smells like bird dirt, definitely needs a good cleaning :) rather than that a really historical place to visit however the place feels like a really negative mood.

name: Artesa Vineyards & Winery
address: 1345 Henry Rd, Napa, CA 94559, United States
text: Really good ambiance. Wines are also nice, not the perfect-best one but still a good option in Napa.

name: Castello di Amorosa
address: 4045 St Helena Hwy, Calistoga, CA 94515, United States
text: Castle and winery tour is nice, especially if you are not familiar with the wine production steps. However the wines were under expectations.

name: V. Sattui Winery
address: 1111 White Ln, St Helena, CA 94574, United States
text: Ambiance is perfect but the wines are not too good.

name: Grand Canyon West Skywalk
address: 808 Eagle Point Rd, Peach Springs, AZ 86434, United States
text: View is just perfect, definitely a must to do in lifetime especially if you are in the neighborhood. However owners made the place as touristic as possible. In addition to that you cannot enter the place with your phone as they say security precautions but just they are selling the photos that take inside with photographers with 65$. They even closed windows in the giftshop so no other people can see it or go to the restaurant upside for the view if you do not pay a meal of 25 per person. So while the experience is perfect, place is just money trap.

text: We ordered delivery, the chicken shish and adana were decent but the beyti was burnt and the lahmacun was brought completely uncooked. The tomatoes in the salad on the side were moldy.

name: KEY Museum
text: Having a museum like this in our country is an incredible blessing. Even if you're not a car enthusiast, it's an amazing museum just for general knowledge. Very meticulous work and collection. I definitely think they lack advertising though.

name: Léone Patisserie & Boulangerie - Urla
address: Hacı İsa, Necati Cumalı Cd. No:17 35430, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: Can a place really have every single product be amazing? Their cakes, desserts, and quiches are absolutely outstanding. Even though the customer crowd can be a bit pretentious, it's also a perfect spot for takeout. Price-wise it may seem expensive at first, but compared to the quality and taste it's actually reasonable and worth every penny.

name: Marmaris Castle and Archeology Museum
text: Not a very big castle but worth seeing if you're in the area. The view from the very top is beautiful.

name: Dr. Gelato
address: Kemeraltı, Org. Mustafa Muğlalı Cd. No:62, 48700 Marmaris/Muğla
text: Amazing ice cream. Especially if you love dark chocolate, do not skip their bitter chocolate flavor. Last year I thought prices were steep but they don't seem to have passed on recent price hikes much. At a place where some crappy beach charges 100 TL for 3 scoops, 10 TL per scoop here is definitely great value as of July 2022.

name: Kosem Doner House
text: Mediocre. Tourist-fleecing joint.

name: Tiki Beach
text: Compared to the quality of surrounding places, this feels like an oasis in the desert. Food quality isn't top-tier for the prices, but I'd say it's the most decent spot in Icmeler. The live music acts are pretty fun too.

name: Jasmine Marmaris
text: The hotel rooms are nice but unfortunately due to its location there's an unbelievable noise problem. It's smack in the middle of 3 nightclubs that put on shows for British tourists and when you're in your room you hear the sound as if you're inside one of those venues until 2 AM. Honestly I'd expect to be informed about this when booking. Especially not suitable at all for families with kids.

name: Amyntas Rock Tombs
text: Definitely don't be lazy about climbing up, it's absolutely stunning.

name: Karmylassos ruins

name: Hertz Car Rental
text:
They took more than 300 euros for just a little scratch which we really doubt as our fault. If you planning to rent a car from here: 1- don’t 2- if you do not have other choice then take the supercover or 3- take photos of the car as many as you can

When returning, staff working there does not know English well and they said it is ok and no charge would be extra so we signed the document since it was German we didnt understand anything and in the end they charged extra fee. Do not sign anything before understanding :)

Luckily there was another room empty and we changed our room. 3 points for the employee who made that change. But the hotel was full and we were lucky too. So if you plan to stay in here, at least not take standard rooms :)

name: Gio Rooftop
address: Double TREE Hilton, Türkmen, Atatürk Blv. No:5, 09400 Kuşadası/Aydın
text: Amazing atmosphere and view, staff are super attentive. For the first time in a long while, we actually had a properly made cocktail in Turkey. They made the Lynchburg with the original recipe and didn't skimp on ingredients. No need to go anywhere else in Kusadasi.

name: DoubleTree by Hilton Kusadasi
address: Atatürk Blv, Yat Limanı Karşısı, 09400 Kuşadası/Aydın
text: Location is great, rooms are quite clean and modern, staff are attentive. But especially at night there's unfortunately way too much noise. There's an insulation problem with adjacent rooms. All the sound comes through. Also the mini-fridges in the rooms are incredibly loud; makes sleeping impossible.

Venue quality is great, beautifully designed and staff are really high quality. But what makes a restaurant a restaurant is unfortunately lacking, and that's flavor in the food. They supposedly do an Italian concept but the pizza we ate was totally Turkish-style :)

name: Kırıntı Bebek
address: Bebek, Cevdet Paşa Cd. No:35, 34342 Beşiktaş/İstanbul

name: Bulvar 216 The Hunger
text: Hello, thank you for your evaluation.😊 You are always welcome🙏🏻 …

name: HUS Şarapçılık
text: The wines are great value for money, we especially loved the emir wine. The crispy parmesan they serve on the side is really well done :)

name: Perdix Şarapçılık
text:
We went on a beautiful Sunday. I can't remember the last time I saw a crowd like that :) definitely worth going on calmer days, weekdays if possible. Getting in and out of the parking lot was really tough with the crowd. There were no seats at the tables but we'd come for a tasting anyway and that was available. The person doing the tasting was very attentive, bless them, gave us plenty of information. Wine quality is generally good, prices might be a touch high; I'd say these are wines that target the premium segment rather than value-for-money. Absolutely worth going, seeing, and tasting. Especially the bogazkere 2018 and cabernet sauvignon 2019 were really impressive.

Update after our second visit: This time we got to eat as well. The pappardelle was truly excellent, we had two plates. The barrel-aged cabernet sauvignon 2019 was genuinely an incredible flavor.

name: Ezel şaraplari
address: Selcen, Çal Güney Yolu No:90, 20765 Çal/Denizli
text: Average flavor. Mostly table wines.

name: Akın Pide Salonu

text: No indoor seating, if you're getting takeout it's best to call ahead and order. They've stopped doing winter service.

name: Travertines of Pamukkale
text: Definitely one of the places anyone should see before they die.

name: Hierapolis
text: You definitely need to set aside the entire full day for the whole area together with Pamukkale. We could barely finish the museums, ancient theater, and nearby structures in half a day. If you also plan to get in the pool and visit the church at the very top, even a full day might not be enough. It's spread across a huge area. If you have time, spreading it over two days would be more comfortable.

name: Laodicea Ancient City
text: Absolutely must-see, worth setting aside at least 2-3 hours.

name: Hara Atlı Spor Kulübü
text: A professional club, the instructors are good. Price-wise it's reasonable compared to alternatives.

name: Old Sailors
