text: Nice venue, we sat down for burgers. It wasn't bad but not at a level where you'd call it legendary either. Nothing that really sticks with you.

name: Cesme Castle
text: Amazing structure, we had no idea about it and stumbled upon it by chance while walking around; we were ashamed of our ignorance. If you're in Cesme, definitely stop by.

name: Urlice Vineyards
text:
The wines are average (could be specific to the vintage we tasted, might get better with a bit more aging) but they especially need to work on communication and service for the restaurant side. At the tasting session we went to with a reservation, we waited about 15 minutes because other guests were late. During this wait, nobody came to inform us (we went and asked ourselves twice), nor did anyone come to ask if we needed anything. (It's really strange for a customer to sit at a table for nearly 20-25 minutes without a waiter even bringing a menu)

On a previous visit, we'd ordered 4 different glasses of wine thinking let's try as many different wines as possible; when we asked for extra empty glasses so we could share with our friends, they told us they don't offer that kind of service and that's not how they operate. A bit of a strange establishment :) unfortunately there's a bit of smugness and pretentiousness going on.

3 stars for the beauty of the setting and especially in honor of the effort put into winemaking & the lady who gave us detailed information about the wines during the tasting.

name: Fermaki
address: İskele, İskele Cd. No:97 D:B, 35441 Urla/İzmir
text: This season they have live music every Friday and the vocalist's voice is really beautiful. Having a table right by the sea is absolutely wonderful, the view is superb. The food is quality. There are some areas for improvement in service but even as it is, it's a lovely setting to have a good time.

name: Mozaik Şarapçılık / Mozaik Winery
text:
I'm not sure there's another place that offers wines this aged at such reasonable prices. All their products are definitely very high quality. I didn't feel a standout where I'd say 'this one is incredible' but the Marselan stood out a bit more on the nose, and the Ekigania was good on the palate.

The venue itself is very simple. There's a small building for tastings and no food service at all. It's worth going with the awareness that you're just there for a tasting.

The horses look beautiful and the grounds are very spacious, makes you wish they'd set up a system where regular people could ride too.

name: Yalovali Kardesler Sarkuteri
address: Nizam, Şht. Recep Koç Cd. No:2, 34970 Adalar/İstanbul
text:
In summer, absolutely do not leave without trying the potato croquettes.
As for deli products, their buffalo cream and dried meats are excellent.

name: Dolci
address: Büyükada-Nizam Mah, 23 Nisan Cd. No:4B, 34970 Adalar/İstanbul
text: The tahini cookies are incredible. Their simits and products in general are high quality too.

name: Buyukada Sariyer Borekcisi
address: Maden, Şht. Recep Koç Cd. 42 A, 34970 Adalar/İstanbul
text: Might be the most amazing börekçi in the world. The ground meat börek is the addictive kind :)

name: Fıçı
text: Kokorec is great, draft beers are fresh. If you're on the island, don't skip it.

name: Pyrgos Otel Restaurant
address: Burgazada, Gezinti Yolu Cd. no:45, 34975 Burgazada/İstanbul
text:
All the mezes are excellent, the view is superb, the staff are very attentive. Definitely don't leave without trying the grilled calamari, it's the house specialty.

As a hotel it's also quite affordable with a great view, you can stay with total peace of mind.

name: İsabey Bağevi
address: İstasyon Mevkii, Gölcükler, 901. Sk. No:38, 35470 Menderes/İzmir
text: Very warm and lovely atmosphere. In their boutique you get 2 free glasses + the rest are priced per glass from their open wines for tasting. We honestly didn't know Sevilen had this many wine varieties. We'd mostly seen their table wines; we were really impressed. The 2016 Plato - Kalecik Karası is absolutely delicious. We also really liked their Pinot Noir. The food is great too. Ideally arrive by 5:30 PM at the latest, browse the boutique first, then sit down for dinner.

name: Hiç Kahvehanesi
text: We usually order delivery. The burgers are truly delicious and they mostly arrive before getting cold. We decided to eat in and luckily they'd moved to a new spot, though the new place seems to have a bit of a ventilation problem. It really smells like food inside, but that seems fixable down the road. In summer, eating outside on-site or ordering delivery seem like the best options. Portions are really filling, the Hiç Ala Burger is a killer :)

name: Pizzeria Luna Romana
address: Urla Plaza, Hacı İsa, 75. Yıl Cumhuriyet Cd. 31F, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: Their tiramisu is absolutely perfect. Pizzas are great too but sometimes they arrive cold because of the long distance, you need to either heat them up or eat there. The quality of ingredients is very obvious, worth the price.

name: Park Inn by Radisson Istanbul Airport, Odayeri
text: Despite being a Radisson, the rooms feel a bit dated. When we got to our room at 1 AM we realized the heating wasn't working. Turned out it was a hotel-wide issue and we had to freeze for about an hour. Luckily the staff were very attentive and managed to fix it, saved us from getting pneumonia :)

name: 101 Bistro
text: Best fish & chips in the world! Far more better than comparing with English ones, definitely worth to try :)

name: Hallgrimskirkja
text: Really liked the minimalism. Organ is also super impressive. View from the top is awesome indeed.

name: Restaurant Suður-Vík
text: Great design, good food. Salad was very nice.

name: Blue Lagoon
text: Cold in winter, especially in the afternoon-evenings. Could be not the best idea :) otherwise, perfect view.

name: Lava Show in Vík
text: It was totally informative, scientific and fascinating performance! Thought as a touristic activity at first but seeing an actual lava was awesome.

name: Black Beach Restaurant
text: Fish was nice, soup was super big (be careful if you are ordering as pre-meal, it could be shared by two) and desserts are super huge (it could be shared by three :))

name: Reynisfjara Beach
text:
One of the most mystical places in the world. Just awesome.

Waves are a little bit dangerous so be careful!

name: Sólheimajökull
text: One of the most peaceful places in the world. Climbing into it also one of the best experiences in my life. Will do it again in the future!

name: Dyrhólaey Lighthouse
text: Awesome view point, a must place to visit.

name: Hótel Kría
text:
Nice hotel. Good design, perfect view, lovely and quick staff. Check in was like only in a second.
Only problem in the rooms are bathroom doors are not actually normal doors so a little off privacy in terms of sound :)

Restaurant was also nice. 5 stars to that.

name: Svínafellsjökull Glacier
text: Unbelievable place

name: Fosshotel Glacier Lagoon
text: Had stopped for restaurant with a group reservation for 15. Got pumpkin soup + salmon and all is perfect. Staff was quick and nice. Place is fancy.

name: Diamond Beach
text: Just amazing! Definitely out from the world :)

name: Skógafoss
text: We got this place in a very bad weather situation and really undecided to go into the top. However I can say that after 15 min of walk, it was definitely worth to see the view from top. A recommendation would be going nearest possible to the waterfall from downside, better view than the top.

name: Gönguleið um Eldhraun
text: From another planet :)

name: Gljúfrabúi
text: Experience inside of the waterfall is awesome. Definitely a place to visit in any season.

name: Center Hotels Plaza
text: Location is perfect, breakfast is between ok and good. Staff is good but hotel in general is old. Within a few renovation it would be better.

name: Þingvallavegur parking viewpoint
text: A good stop for photos in winter, especially on a sunny day with snow on the ground.

name: Forréttabarinn
text: Awesome food, loved everything we had: duck salad, quesadilla and fish of the day. Perfect staff!

name: Kerið Crater
text: Just amazing! View from top side is perfect, definitely full path should be leaded around the crater.

name: Gullfoss Falls
text: A perfect nature, definitely a place to visit. Went in winter and unfortunately the path was closed which is really near to the waterfall. But still worth to see in real world!

name: Geysir
text: Definitely a place to add to the tour program. Sorry for not seeing the big geysir exploitation due to it is not active anymore, it is still nice to see small one active and alive.

name: Skogafoss Bistro Bar
text: Good place, quick and helpful staff + fast service. Salmon was very tasty.

name: Gullfoss - Panorama Restaurant - Cafe - Shop
text: Really nice staff and very quick service. We had a meat soup as a group. While service is awesome, soup taste was not so good. Too much salt and cabbage flavor :)

name: Le KocK
text:
Perfect burgers, loved them! Definitely worth to try.
For drinks if you want to drink a beer, choosing draft one would be more make sense than the bottled ones in terms of both taste and price.

name: Halldórskaffi
text: We came to the restaurant at 7:30 as a 15 people of a group and had our dinner at 9:15!! They even gave us the water after half an hour. Our tour guide take the orders because staff was too busy. Definitely not a good place for big groups because they do not have capacity of staff for that. After waiting so long, taste of food would be not too important.

name: DorukKaya Ski & Mountain Resort
text:
The rooms have unfortunately become dated compared to the rest of the hotel, not worth what you pay. Especially the 5th floor rooms, they look like suites split in two. You pay regular room price but essentially hear the sounds from the other room as if you're sharing it; what should normally be a family room they've locked off with a door that's basically a bathroom door. They gave no information about this either, so if you stay, definitely tell them you don't want one of these rooms.

The hotel was uncomfortable and very crowded, but being able to access the slopes directly from the hotel is definitely very convenient, and the buffet and food are genuinely good. The only silly thing is how they've restricted the all-inclusive concept. Soda is free and unlimited during meals, but if you go out to the lobby and want a soda, it costs money. Because of this alone, nobody leaves the restaurant during meal hours and it creates extra crowding. The restaurant unfortunately doesn't have enough capacity for this kind of crowd.

name: Sardis Ancient City
text:
We spotted it by chance from a sign on the Izmir-Manisa road, researched it, then stopped by. Call it our ignorance or a symptom of our country's lack of promotion, but we only learned about this historical gem by coincidence. It's absolutely a gorgeous ancient city that should be seen in person. It's still under restoration and construction, but in about 5-6 years it likely has the potential to become a tourist area on the level of Ephesus.

A bit further inside the village there's the Artemis temple section. If you're already here, we recommend stopping by there too.

name: Kula Peri Bacaları
text: If you're in the area, definitely stop by. It's not as expansive or historically rich as Cappadocia, but the fairy chimneys themselves are absolutely a natural wonder worth seeing. The only downside is the Cappadocia-wannabe name :)

name: Tarihi Kula Evleri
address: Zaferiye, Şht. Yüzbaşı Salih Sk. No:48, 45170 Kula/Manisa
text: We went based on the abundance of positive reviews. Don't bother. Very little of it has been restored, most of it is in ruins, there's nothing appealing about it. Especially in winter months, you'll be breathing pure coal smoke. If this were abroad, it could attract millions of tourists a year, but unfortunately due to mismanagement it's basically just waiting to completely collapse. I hope it gets a proper restoration soon and is reclaimed for our country.

name: Villa Estet by Anemon Yanıkülke bağ otel
address: Merkez Mah. Atatürk Cad. No: 317 İzmir-Ankara Devlet Karayolu, 45170 Kula/Manisa
text:
We went for a wine tasting and ended up staying one night. First of all, the vineyards and venue are absolutely superb, and the wines, especially the whites, are excellent. We were particularly impressed by the Muscat and Sauvignon Blanc grapes. The cellars and production facilities are also great.

Beyond that, the restaurant is average, and don't set your expectations too high for the accommodation. If you're a wine lover, we absolutely recommend it. Otherwise, for a fine dining or a great accommodation experience, it's a bit debatable for the price.

name: Nif Bağları ve Şaraphanesi
address: Sarıceviz Mevkii, Yeni Mahalle No:25 D:2, Bağyurdu, 35743 Kemalpaşa/İzmir
text: We went for a wine tasting. The venue is absolutely beautiful, the staff are attentive. They don't charge for the wine tasting and the staff are as helpful as can be. The wines are very distinctive, though the 2020 vintage reds were generally a bit light. The Bornova Muscat was very successful. The 2012 vintage Shiraz Reserve was excellent. As of February 2022, they're also selling at incredible discounts with a current promotion, so we'd recommend it to everyone.

name: MMG winery
address: Bademler, Gödence Yolu Cd. No:40, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: Their white wines in particular are excellent, and the venue's view is stunning. The owners are very friendly and helpful. A true value-for-money spot. We were really impressed by the Bornova Muscat and the Viognier-Chardonnay blend. We'll definitely visit again when their new vintages come out.

name: Ayda Bağları Winery & Vineyards
address: Gödence, Kuruçeşme Küme Evler Sk No. 39, 35460 Seferihisar/İzmir
text: We visited for a wine tasting. Unfortunately in terms of value for money, they fall well short compared to other wineries in the area. Prices are high, and the wine they poured into tasting glasses was quite little. We weren't impressed at an 'oh this is really good' level by any of their wines. The venue itself is definitely nice, maybe the food or accommodation are good (we didn't try those) but we wouldn't recommend going just for a wine tasting.

name: Hotel Demas City
text: Good location in city centre (but not in a good district), a decent city hotel with good designed rooms. Toilet door was a little bit weird + no slippers in the rooms + a little bit dusty. Good for the price.

name: ZWICKL - Gastlichkeit am Viktualienmarkt
text: Love the Paulaner Zwickl version!!

name: Hofbräuhaus München
text: A little bit expensive comparing to other restaurants nearby. Traditional German restaurant which is a good experience for especially tourists but in Christmas it was too crowded and stuffy. Could be a little bit risky at covid.

name: Cheese & More by Henri Willig
text: Best cheese ever! Loving the young goat one. All they need is a little wine serving when tasting on the cheese :)

name: Das Moor - Fränkische Spezialitäten
text: Expensive comparing quality of the food. Fancy looking restaurant especially with Christmas decoration but not exactly worth in terms of taste.

name: Café PIQUE NIQUE
text: Awesome bagels!! Good for a quick breakfast.

name: Cafe & Bar Celona Nürnberg
text: Had tapas with potatoes, meatballs, cheese and shrimps which all was nice. Music is good and feels like a night club too. Good for Saturday nights.

name: SORAT Hotel Saxx Nuremberg
text: Great location, fancy and clean rooms. Only negative thing was they do not have slippers on their rooms so having on your own could be good idea. Nights could be cold in winter so increasing the temperature could be a nice idea too.

name: AKIN'IN YERİ BALIK RESTAURANT
text: Amazing original mezes, a must-visit. Especially the sea bass lokum and the flavor ball are addictively good, don't leave without trying them :) We've been there at least 5-6 times by now and not a single guest of ours has ever disliked it. Truly a restaurant that's nailed the seafood meze game. Should definitely be on everyone's must-try-before-you-die list.

name: Urla Vino Locale
text: One of the best chef restaurants in the area. We've been at least 4-5 times and I can say we were never disappointed. Although the price is a bit high, it's absolutely worth it for what they offer. You might just find the answer to the question 'how good can a squash be' in their summer menus :)

name: ALKANLAR Kasap Kebap
text: You really shouldn't say you've had kokoreç until you've tried it here. Friends who'd been were singing its praises, so we went with sky-high expectations and it genuinely didn't disappoint. I can say it's an incredible flavor. We had the portion. The Babadağ kebab is also quite tasty, but I'm not sure I'd order it again when kokoreç is an option :) Definitely a must-try. Those who gave low scores mostly complained about the price. The ambiance might be rough around the edges but it serves you a taste you won't find even at the fanciest restaurant. They make all their meats themselves so what they eat and drink etc. is known, and it's all reportedly natural. If you're the type who likes paying for fancy venues, you might not enjoy it. If flavor matters to you, try it no matter what.

name: Quick China Çayyolu
address: Prof. Dr. Ahmet Taner Kışlalı, 2864. Cd. No:15 D:C, 06810 Çankaya/Ankara
text: The dishes and sushi we had were quite good, the menu is very extensive. The ambiance is wonderful. Only the hot and sour soup fell just a notch below our expectations, other than that everything was more than good.

name: Tafali Bağcılık Butik Şaraphane
text: We were really on the fence about going to Kalecik because it didn't look like much on the maps. When we searched for Tomurcukbağ we didn't find what we expected, and when we searched for this place a very sweet lady picked up the phone. Since the drive from central Ankara also takes quite a while, we were very hesitant. Especially when we first arrived and saw the entrance, we almost felt let down. But after seeing the setup and tasting the wines, our whole opinion changed. One of those places where you can say 'so glad we went.' It opened our horizons about winemaking. We got to see how quality work can be done without massive investments. Big thanks to Alime Hanım and her sibling for that. If you're into winemaking at all, I absolutely recommend stopping by.

name: Kavaklıdere Sineması
address: Barbaros, Tunalı Hilmi Cd. No:105 D:A, 06700 Çankaya/Ankara
text: Even just seeing its sign is so emotional. The cinema where my childhood was spent. I hope it reopens so we can walk inside again one day.

name: Kav Butik
address: Kavaklıdere, Tunus Cd No:88 D:B, 06680 Çankaya/Ankara
text: We finally found their sweet fortified wines that we'd been searching for for years, so we're very happy about that. We'd recommend both the white and the red to everyone. It would be much better if they offered a tasting in my opinion. They ship to your address which is a plus. Their boutiques in Izmir and Istanbul had closed, finding this place was a godsend. The Pendore series is also recommended.

name: Anıtkabir
text: When we went on November 10th the atmosphere was truly very emotional. We hope to go on October 29th next year and be there in a more festive spirit.

name: En Moda Cafe
text: We went for breakfast, the taste was more than sufficient. I think their location is marked incorrectly, we had trouble meeting up with people who were coming :) The only problem we had was getting attitude from the waiter. While paying the bill, as we were doing the 'who's going to pay' thing, he came over and snapped at us saying 'why don't you decide among yourselves and then call me' and walked off :) My cousin's reaction was 'I haven't even gotten this kind of attitude from my husband until now.' We'll chalk it up to his youth. Seems like it's not well known, on Sundays when you can't find a spot for breakfast in Moda, it could be a nice alternative. But the location does seem wrong, you might have trouble finding it :)

name: Pişi Mutfak Moda – Kahvaltı & Brunch
text: The food was tasty but honestly it's a bit ridiculous to charge an extra service fee when three people will eat a spread breakfast that's meant for two. We had a two-person breakfast among 4 people + extra pişi and an omelette on the side; even then I'd say we couldn't finish it. So the portions are more than generous. The extra service fee thing really isn't cool though. Charging just for plates and cutlery because one more person is eating the same food is kind of pointless.

name: Gusina
text: The breakfast options are really well done. Usually it's hard to find something you'd call healthy when eating out, but this place was more than good. They don't have alcohol for evening hangouts yet but they said the license is in progress. Just be aware that the posters and decor might give the impression it's a bar :)

name: Çakıl Meyhane
address: Bostancı Mahallesi Hatboyu Çk. Sk. &, Bostancı, Prof. Dr. Ali Nihat Tarlan Cd, 34744 Kadıköy
text: The liver and the Çakıl special kebab are truly the addictive kind. We eat the mezes alongside just to take the edge off their impact :) A place we're regulars at that has never let its quality slip. The only thing is it can get a bit too smoky for non-smokers.

name: Brio İtalian Restaurant
text: The food is genuinely good, the ambiance is well done, the prices aren't outrageous. The only thing we didn't find impressive was their wine selection. At a restaurant of this caliber, you'd expect someone who could recommend wines based on what we're eating or who knows the wines' profiles better. In trying different wines, the Chianti and Antioche we picked were a bit disappointing. Other than that, I'd say everything was excellent.

name: KÖSTEM ZEYTİNYAĞI MÜZESİ
text:
How wonderful that a museum like this exists in our country. Huge thanks to everyone who contributed to its creation. For anyone interested, it's an incredibly detailed museum. There are also exhibits that can capture children's attention.

The olive oils are superb. We'd bought a small bottle to try, but I think from now on we'll be getting most of our olive oil from here. It's genuinely excellent :)

name: Çeşmealtı Windsurf Ve Yelken Kulübü
address: No:, Denizli, Yalı Cd. No:63, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: This is where we learned windsurfing. Prices are a bit higher compared to other places (like Foca) but the location and surroundings are decent. The instructors are very young but total pros. If you're curious, we definitely recommend taking the lessons. It's a lot of fun.

name: Fırın Vourla
text: The subye dolma and chocolate brownies are absolutely phenomenal. Worth going back again and again just for those. Their wine menu is very extensive and lovely, and the fact that it's heavy on Denizli wines is a nice touch. There are some hiccups in service but the flavor and value for money make up for the hiccups. Going during less busy hours/days would make for an even happier experience.

name: Levan
text: The mussel pasta is incredible! Ambiance is gorgeous, service is great too. We'd been putting off going because we heard they'd shifted mostly to pizza in the last year, but after going we totally regretted not coming sooner :) Truly very original flavors, worth trying.

name: Urla Sarapcilik
text: The venue is absolutely perfect, you feel like you're in Hollywood. The wines, especially Tempus, are very good, but if you start comparing value for money, you can find much higher quality wines on the market. Definitely worth going for the venue, and the tasting fees are reasonable and worth joining; but think twice before buying bottles I'd say :)

name: Rosole Ristorante Italiano
address: Bedesten Pasajı, Atatürk, Aşıklar Cd., 35680 Foça/İzmir
text: It was closed when we went at 8 PM on a Sunday. Worth calling ahead to confirm before going. Despite their listing saying they're only closed on Mondays, they were also closed on Sunday and there was no notice posted.

name: Yenifoça Windsurfing & Sailing Sports Club
text: We had an absolute blast in the single session lesson we took. The instructors are very attentive, we got into stand-up windsurfing and it sparked our interest :) A great activity to try out.

name: La Petra Otel & Kahvaltı Evi, Pasta Cafe, Restorant
address: 35680, Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, Ali Stair Cd. No:19, 35687 Foça/İzmir
text: Hotel services were fine but the sound insulation between rooms is unfortunately zero. We had to sleep and wake up to the sounds of the person next door coughing up phlegm and using the toilet. If you're sensitive to noise, I'd recommend not staying here.

name: Manal Koyu
text: An amazing cove. The calm and not-too-deep sea is perfect for families with kids.

name: Çeşme Bazlama Kahvaltı - Alaçatı
text: We'd been to their Istanbul locations before, came here too and the service is still the same quality, speed, and charm. On busy days you might wait in line a bit, but since they don't serve coffee etc., people generally leave once they finish their breakfast :)

name: İyi Resto Bar
text: A perfect and well-executed spot for a snack-style concept.

We chose it because it was one of the highest-rated beaches, but it was a complete disappointment. We're not complaining about the high prices or anything like that because we went in knowing what to expect. The real problem is what you get for the money you pay.

name: Alaçatı Selin Otel
text: A nice and clean establishment. No issues whatsoever aside from the rooms not having windows.

name: OD Urla
address: Süt Pınarı Mevkii, Rüstem, 2018/9 Sokak No:28, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: Unfortunately an overhyped bubble. The first time we went, the sauces on the meats gave us heartburn all evening. On our second visit, even though we hadn't mentioned this to our guests at all, they experienced the same problem. On top of that, there's no alternative dish besides meat/chicken/fish. The only thing we were happy with flavor-wise was their cocktails with lamb's ear. The service etc. is genuinely excellent, but the food content unfortunately doesn't meet expectations.

name: Rüzgar Gülü & Zeytin Kafe
text: Their böreks are amazing, a true family-run place :)

name: Şirince Artemis Restaurant ve Şarap evi
address: Eski Okul Binası, Şirince Mahallesi Şirince Artemis Restaurant ve Şarap evi 220 sokak, Taş Mektep Müzesi No: 7, 35920 Selçuk/İzmir
text: A standard restaurant, don't have very high expectations.
The cube sour cherry mistel and pomegranate wines are a must-try.

name: Sahil Restaurant
text: The view is gorgeous, the seafood is genuinely excellent. We especially loved the caviar pasta and the sailor's börek, and the octopus was great too. The seafood şevketi bostan is truly a real specialty. When we went last year the staff seemed a bit indifferent, but on this year's visit we were glad to see they'd improved on that as well. Try to go on less crowded days.

name: 1774 Kazdağı Termal Butik Otel
text: The staff are friendly, the rooms are spacious and clean, breakfast comes to your room at the time you choose with the contents you've selected. It's one of the smaller hotels in the area compared to others you could pick. Just note there's no alcohol, come knowing that :)

name: Assos Ancient City
text: Definitely go all the way down and make sure to walk toward the section with the wall. We thought there was only the ancient theater below but a whole city appeared before us and we were shocked. If they cleaned it up properly, it would draw millions of tourists a year.

name: Halici Han
text: We had one of our dinners at this hotel. The hotel owners make the food themselves. We were very pleased with our meal of olive oil dishes and meze-heavy fare, we'd recommend it to everyone.

name: Kadırga Beach
text: The cove is absolutely beautiful, we didn't expect the sea to be this nice. Just a bit cold but perfect for hot days. The businesses there are honestly mediocre, but the crowd is decent with no obnoxious types, so that's a plus :) Just don't make the mistake of ordering fried calamari at one of the places there, stick to fries at most :)

name: İdasos Taş Odalar
text: Spacious and nicely decorated rooms. It's a family-run place, they're very warm and helpful. Breakfast is tasty, location is great. If I stay in Behram again I'll definitely choose this place.

name: Sed Restoran
text: Food is average taste-wise but reasonable when you consider the value. The setting and ambiance are particularly nice though.

name: Asmadan Bağcılık ve Şarapçılık
text:
We spotted it from their sign on our way to Suvla and since the place looked really nice from outside we figured let's give it a shot. The fiasco started right at the entrance: security takes down the first and last name of everyone who enters. This could be asked for HES code checks, but when one person's code per family is enough, why take other people's names? On top of that, they were all directly recorded. The vibe of being catalogued while going to a restaurant is not pleasant.

Beyond that, despite telling us on the phone they have an a la carte menu, when we got there we learned they only had set menus. They said they could serve individual items separately, but the menu has no prices listed, so you have to ask one by one, which is a bit uncomfortable.

Finally, during the tasting, when my wife mentioned she was getting smoky aromas in a wine, the person conducting the tasting made condescending remarks asking whether this was our first time doing a tasting etc., which wasn't cool at all.

Maybe it'll get better in the future, but for now the service is unfortunately mediocre.

name: Hotel Caeli & Caeli Winery
text: Every day at 5 PM you can do a tasting and facility tour for 120 TL. The craftsmanship of the facility is truly something to be proud of for Turkey. We also thought their wines would be more expensive but they're reasonably priced given the ambiance. We'd recommend it to everyone.

name: Вип Комплекс
text:
No English speaking staff. Photos are completely different from the website. Too expensive comparing to similar places around.

Edit to owner reply: We actually love the Dospat Lake and the ambiance of the place is just awesome. Dospat is one of my favorite places in the world. But that is not enough to make this hotel good. It needs a good renovation or should change the price strategy.

name: Son Vapur
text: Excellent food and mezes. The okra octopus and the fish simit are truly phenomenal. We'll definitely come back in the future to try other dishes.

name: Barbare Bağları
text: Their wines are genuinely tasty, nice atmosphere. The only thing is the all-inclusive price is a bit much for what you get value-wise. At similar bed-and-breakfast vineyards we've been to, we ate and drank more and paid less. The 2012 Sauvignon Blanc white was phenomenal though.

name: Suvla Wine Factory
text: Being able to try all their wines by the 5 cl glass is an amazing service. Overall the wine and food quality is great. The claret-syrah rosé blend in particular is an unforgettable flavor.

name: Eflin Butik Otel
text: Very sincere owners and staff + the breakfast is organic and everything is very delicious. The only negative was that our room was unfortunately next to the storage and kitchen & its window faced directly onto the street; because of that, every passerby sounded like they were in the room + everything said in the kitchen was like it was inside the room. I wish they had different room prices, because the rooms up top in the open and the room we stayed in cost the same, and if we could've chosen, we'd have picked the upper rooms. If you can choose, definitely go for the upper rooms.

name: Yengec Restaurant
text: They have very original mezes and hot appetizers, but the prices are at least a couple notches more expensive than comparable places.

name: Kefalos Beach
text: The sea was beautiful but unfortunately the mucilage took over :(

name: Uğurlu Plajı
text: Very wavy and full of seaweed, we didn't like it.

name: tuz golu
text: There were flamingos in August!! Incredible :)

name: Dessera Bağ Evi
text: Genuinely quality wines, attentive staff. We were sad we couldn't tour the production facility but they said they'd closed tours due to the pandemic. They also have accommodation.

name: Rahat Tepe
address: TsentarPlovdiv Center, ul. "Doctor Stoyan Chomakov" 18, 4000 Plovdiv, Bulgaria
text: Price is really low comparing to both quantity & quality. View & ambiance is good. Really good wine they produced & have!

name: Rodopski Kat Complex - village of Leshten
text: Great ambiance but worst service. We waited for just cheese plate for 45 mins. Children cried from starving. House wine is not a wine but probably rotten. Maybe can go only to drink a beer for ambiance.

name: Müdavim Palamutbükü
address: Cumalı Mahallesi, Palamutbükü Sokak No:50 Palamutbükü / Datça, 48900 Palamutbükü/Muğla
text: Tasty food, attentive staff, fast service.

name: Almond Palamutbükü
text: The restaurant is really good but the service is a bit slow and glitchy. The octopus is a must-try.

name: Abidin&Dino Kedi köpek oteli
text: A place where you can leave your loved ones with complete peace of mind.

name: Хотел "Хеброс"
address: Staria gradPlovdiv Center, ul. "Konstantin Stoilov" 51, 4000 Plovdiv, Bulgaria
text: Great traditional hotel! Loved the rooms and location. Definitely will stay again here if been Plovdiv again.

name: Garden of Rich
text: Great garden & breakfast but think twice when ordering bruschetta because it is totally different from usual ones :)

name: Yoruk Aile Evi
text: Content is standard, prices are a bit steep for what you get. Don't think I'd go again.

name: Karia Hotel Palamutbükü
address: Cumalı Mah. Cumalı Sk. No : 169/ B, 48900 Datça/Muğla
text: The hotel is nice but despite the staff being well-meaning, they're unfortunately untrained. Between being short-staffed and undertrained, things are really slow and they can't keep up with anything. They said there'd be a shuttle but unfortunately it wasn't available when we needed it. They need a bit more organizational improvement.

name: MAVİ TRANSFER DATÇA
text: Very professional staff, we were very pleased.

name: S. S. ERDEK SOCIETY MARITIME MOTOR CARRIERS
text: The trip they claim takes 2 hours 15 minutes on their website ends up being at least 4 hours once you board the ferry. The toilets are terrible, everyone's maskless as if the pandemic never happened, and even the staff smoke indoors in enclosed areas. Hope to God we never have to use this again.

name: Kabatepe Ferry Pier
text: We went straight to the pier because we'd read in reviews that there's a price difference between online and walk-up tickets. We ended up waiting in line forever. If time matters to you, definitely buy online. When you show up at the gate they treat you like a second-class citizen, and the price difference isn't worth it.

name: Adresin Hotel
address: Kocasinan Mahallesi Dr. Doğan Cihan Cad. No:5, 22000 Edirne
text: Very crowded, breakfast is in an indoor area, a hotel that acts like the pandemic never happened.

name: BAKUCHA Vineyard Hotel & SPA
text: The venue, hotel, ambiance, and wines are all quite nice, but unfortunately that's all. We stayed during a quiet period after the holiday; our main reason for staying was to do a tasting and take a production facility tour, but on the date we visited the wine expert was on leave so we couldn't do either, even though we'd mentioned this was our purpose before booking. The staff are well-meaning but good intentions aren't enough in the hospitality industry. They're genuinely uninformed. In its current state, it gives off the feeling of an abandoned establishment.

name: Gurbuz Winery & Restaurant
address: Yeni Mahalle D100 Karayolu kenari No: 33, C Blok 10-27 No, Buyukkaristiran, 39750 Luleburgaz/Kırklareli
text: We'd read in the reviews that it was in an industrial zone, but we didn't expect it to be this deep in one :) If we hadn't read the reviews, we'd most likely have turned around at the door, but thank goodness we didn't. Genuinely tasty wines and knowledgeable staff. It was a great tasting experience, and we'll definitely try to stop by again to taste different wines in the future.

name: Bağlı Kokoreç
address: Samanlık, Denizli Muğla Yolu No:201, 20500 Tavas/Denizli
text: Incredible flavor!

name: Özbek Akkum Plajı
text: Water is warm, shallow and full of seaweed. It doesn't get deeper no matter how far you walk. Perfect for families with kids.

name: OFF ROAD ATV TOUR
address: İzmir Atv Safari, Rüstem, Koca Dere Yolu Sk no:82/6, 35430 Urla/İzmir
text: An amazing experience! We'd recommend it to everyone, the guide is very attentive and knowledgeable.

name: Hotel Gelibolu
address: Alaeddin, Kore Kahramanlar Cd. No:8, 17500 Gelibolu/Çanakkale
text: The nicest hotel you can stay at in the area, one of the best in terms of value for money. The location is incredible, the hotel owners do everything they can for guest satisfaction. The attic room is a tiny bit low-ceilinged but I think it should definitely be requested. My wife and I stayed 2 nights at the hotel and it had been a long time since we'd stayed at a boutique hotel that cared for us this much.
